fields.description: You gain a +1 bonus to your next Agility Roll.
You can apply this venom to a weapon that deals physical damage to add a d6 to your next damage roll with that weapon.
You gain a +1 bonus to your next Strength Roll.
You can eat these paired leaves to immediately gain 2 Hope.
You gain a +1 bonus to your next Finesse Roll.
When you drink the contents of this vial, you can see in total darkness until your next rest.
You gain a +1 bonus to your next Instinct Roll.
You can make a Finesse Roll to throw this shard at a group of adversaries within Far range. Targets you succeed against take 1d20 magic damage.
You gain a +1 bonus to your next Presence Roll.
You can drink this potion to choose one additional downtime move.
You gain a +1 bonus to your next Knowledge Roll.
You can apply this venom to a weapon that deals physical damage to add a d8 to your next damage roll with that weapon.
Clear 1d4 HP.
Clear 1d4 Stress.
You can spend a Hope to use this clay, altering your face enough to make you unrecognizable until your next rest.
When an adversary attacks you, use this vial and roll a number of d6s equal to your Agility. Add the highest result to your Evasion against the attack.
Eat this root to leap up to Far range once without needing to roll.
Mark a Stress and clear a HP.
Clear 1d4+1 HP.
Clear 1d4+1 Stress.
You can use this stitcher to spend any number of Hope and clear that many Armor Slots.
You can apply this salve to your neck to breathe underwater for a number of minutes equal to your level.
By touching this piece of parchment to another, you can perfectly copy the second parchment's contents. Once used, this parchment becomes mundane paper.
You can make a Finesse Roll to throw this shard at a group of adversaries within Far range. Targets you succeed against take 2d20 magic damage.
You gain a +1 bonus to your Agility until your next rest.
You gain a +1 bonus to your Strength until your next rest.
You gain a +1 bonus to your Finesse until your next rest.
You gain a +1 bonus to your Instinct until your next rest.
You gain a +1 bonus to your Presence until your next rest.
You gain a +1 bonus to your Knowledge until your next rest.
You can drink this blood to disappear from where you are and immediately reappear at a point you can see within Very Far range.
After drinking this potion, the next successful attack you make critically succeeds.
You can apply this saliva to a weapon that deals physical damage to add a d12 to your next damage roll with that weapon.
You can use this stone to take a spell or grimoire from your vault, use it once, and return it to your vault.
You can drink this potion to fall asleep for a full night's rest. You clear all Stress upon waking.
You can eat this meal to clear all HP and Stress and gain 1d4 Hope.
This honey can be used to glue two objects together permanently.
You can drink this potion to halve your size until you choose to drop this form or your next rest. While in this form, you have a +2 bonus to Agility and a -1 penalty to your Proficiency.
You can drink this potion to double your size until you choose to drop this form or your next rest. While in this form, you have a +2 bonus to Strength and a +1 bonus to your Proficiency.
If you die while holding this stone, an ally can take a card from your loadout to place in their loadout or vault. After they take this knowledge, the stone crumbles.
You can consume this moss during a rest to clear 1d10 HP or 1d10 Stress.
You can activate this orb to create a flash of bright light. All targets within Close range become Vulnerable until they mark HP.
After you drink this tea, you instantly kill your target when you critically succeed on an attack. If you don't critically succeed on an attack before your next long rest, you die.
When you take damage, you can spend a Hope to negate that damage, after which the mirror shatters.
You can use this stardrop to summon a hailstorm of comets that deals 8d20 physical damage to all targets within Very Far range.
During downtime, you automatically clear a Stress.
This handcrafted whistle has a distinctive sound. When you blow this whistle, its piercing tone can be heard within a 1-mile radius.
When you succeed on an attack with an arrow stored in this quiver, gain a bonus to the damage roll equal to your current tier.
You can light this magic torch at will. The flame's light fills a much larger space than it should, enough to illuminate a cave bright as day.
This pair of orbs allows any creatures holding them to communicate with each other across any distance.
This pair of locking cuffs comes with a key.
A creature with a Spellcast trait wearing this cloak can adjust its color, texture, and size at will.
You can make a Finesse Roll using this net to trap a small creature. A trapped target can break free with a successful Attack Roll (16).
You can pour out the strange liquid contents of this jar to instantly produce fire. The contents regenerate when you take a long rest.

This flat rod is inscribed with runes. When you activate the rod, it is immediately suspended in place. Until the rod is deactivated, it can't move, doesn't abide by the rules of gravity, and remains in place.
Activate this pebble-sized stone to memorize the appearance of someone you can see. Spend a Hope to magically recreate this guise on yourself as an illusion.
This magical chest appears empty. When you speak a specific trigger word or action and open the chest, you can see the items stored within it.
This case can fit a small animal companion. While the companion is inside, the animal and case are immune to all damage and harmful effects.
Three times per rest when you succeed on an attack with one of these arrows, you can add your Proficiency to the damage roll.

You can attach this stone to armor that doesn't already have a feature. The armor gains the following feature.  Resilient: Before you mark your last Armor Slot, roll a d6. On a result of 6, reduce the severity by one threshold without marking an Armor Slot.
When you use this key to open a locked door, you gain advantage on the Finesse Roll.

Position this prism in a location of your choosing and activate it. All allies within Close range of it gain a +1 bonus to their Spellcast Rolls. While activated, the prism can't be moved. Once the prism is deactivated, it can't be activated again until your next long rest.
As a downtime move, you can use the bone of a creature to craft a Minor Stamina Potion.
As a downtime move, you can use a vial of blood to craft a Minor Health Potion.
These two compasses point toward each other no matter how far apart they are.
This tiny sprite sits in the curve of your ear canal and whispers helpful advice during combat. Once per short rest, you can gain advantage on an attack roll.
You can climb up vertical surfaces and across ceilings.
You can store the name and details of up to three hostile creatures inside this book. You gain a +1 bonus to action rolls against those creatures.
As a downtime move, you can mark a Stress to craft a Vial of Darksmoke.

You can attach this stone to a weapon that doesn't already have a feature. The weapon gains the following feature. Brutal: When you roll the maximum value on a damage die, roll an additional damage die.

You can attach this stone to a weapon that doesn't already have a feature. The weapon gains the following feature. Powerful: On a successful attack, roll an additional damage die and discard the lowest result.
While falling, you can mark a Stress to deploy this small parachute and glide safely to the ground.
Spend a Hope to activate this ring. Your footsteps are silent until your next rest.
When you would mark your last Stress, roll a d6. On a result of 5 or higher, don't mark it.
This flask can hold two different liquids. You can swap between them by flipping a small switch on the flask's side.

You can convince this small bag of sand to be much heavier or lighter with a successful Presence Roll (10). Additionally, on a successful Finesse Roll (10), you can blow a bit of sand into a target's face to make them temporarily Vulnerable.
Once per long rest, you can activate this ring after a successful attack against you to halve the damage.
If you have at least one Phoenix Feather on you when you fall unconscious, you gain a +1 bonus to the roll you make to determine whether you gain a scar.

Once per long rest, you can open this small box and roll a d12. On a result of 1–6, it's empty. On a result of 7–10, it contains one random common consumable. On a result of 11–12, it contains two random common consumables.
You can attach this charm to a weapon with a Melee range. Three times per rest, you can activate the charm and attack a target within Close range.

You can plant this seed in the ground to grow a portal in that spot. The portal is ready to use in 24 hours. You can use this portal to travel to any other location where you planted a portal seed. A portal can be destroyed by dealing any amount of magic damage to it.

As a downtime move, you can meditate on an ideal or principle you hold dear and focus your will into this chain. Once per long rest, you can spend a Hope to roll a d20 as your Hope Die for rolls that directly align with that principle.
Once per long rest, you can activate this amulet to become Hidden until you move. While Hidden in this way, you remain unseen even if an adversary moves to where they would normally see you.

During a long rest, if you have 6 Hope, you can spend a Hope to imbue this locket with your bountiful resolve. When you have 0 Hope, you can use the locket to immediately gain a Hope. The locket must be re-imbued before it can be used this way again.
When you store items in this bag, they are kept in a pocket dimension that never runs out of space. You can retrieve an item at any time.
You gain a +1 bonus to your Agility. You can only carry one relic.
You gain a +1 bonus to your Strength. You can only carry one relic.
You gain a +1 bonus to your Finesse. You can only carry one relic.
You gain a +1 bonus to your Instinct. You can only carry one relic.
You gain a +1 bonus to your Presence. You can only carry one relic.
You gain a +1 bonus to your Knowledge. You can only carry one relic.
You gain a +1 bonus to an Experience of your choice. You can only carry one relic.
While you carry this pendant, your weapons with a Melee range that deal physical damage have a gossamer sheen and can attack targets within Very Close range.
You can walk on the surface of water as if it were soft ground.

When you sculpt this ball of clay into a clay animal companion, it behaves as that animal. For example, a clay spider can spin clay webs, while a clay bird can fly. The clay companion retains memory and identity across different shapes, but they can adopt new mannerisms with each form.
As a downtime move, you can use a handful of fine gold dust to craft Mythic Dust.
Once per long rest, you can spend 2 Hope to recall a domain card from your vault instead of paying its Recall Cost.
You can attach this gem to a weapon, allowing you to use your Agility when making an attack with that weapon.
You can attach this gem to a weapon, allowing you to use your Strength when making an attack with that weapon.
You can attach this gem to a weapon, allowing you to use your Finesse when making an attack with that weapon.
You can attach this gem to a weapon, allowing you to use your Instinct when making an attack with that weapon.
You can attach this gem to a weapon, allowing you to use your Presence when making an attack with that weapon.
You can attach this gem to a weapon, allowing you to use your Knowledge when making an attack with that weapon.
Once per session, when the GM spends a Fear, you can spend 4 Hope to cancel the effects of that spent Fear.
Once per session, you can spend 5 Hope to lead a Tag Team Roll with three PCs instead of two.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 2 > Merchant Baron
entries.name: Merchant Baron

fields.description: An accomplished merchant with a large operation under their command.
fields.motives: Abuse power, gather resources, mobilize minions

features.name: Everyone Has a Price
features.description: Spend a Fear to off er a target a dangerous bargain for something they want or need. If used on a PC, they must make a Presence Reaction Roll (17). On a failure, they must mark 2 Stress or take the deal.

features.name: The Best Muscle Money Can Buy
features.description: Once per scene, mark a Stress to summon 1d4+1 Tier 1 adversaries, who appear at Far range, to enforce the Baron’s will.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 2 > Minotaur Wrecker
entries.name: Minotaur Wrecker

fields.description: A massive bull-headed fi rbolg with a quick temper.

features.name: Ramp Up
features.description: You must spend a Fear to spotlight the Minotaur. While spotlighted, they can make their standard attack against all targets within range.

features.name: Charging Bull
features.description: Mark a Stress to charge through a group within Close range and make an attack against all targets in the Minotaur’s path. Targets the Minotaur succeeds against take

features.name: Gore
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Very Close range, moving the Minotaur into Melee range of them. On a success, deal 2d8 direct physical damage.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 2 > Mortal Hunter
entries.name: Mortal Hunter

fields.description: An undead fi gure wearing a heavy leather coat, with searching eyes and a casually cruel demeanor.
fields.attack: +5 | Tear at Flesh: Very Close | 2d12+1 phy

features.name: Terrifying
features.description: When the Hunter makes a successful attack, all PCs within Far range lose a Hope and you gain a Fear.

features.name: Deathlock
features.description: Spend a Fear to curse a target within Very Close range with a necrotic Deathlock until the end of the scen Attacks made by the Hunter against a Deathlocked target deal direct damage. The Hunter can only maintain one Deathlock at a time.

features.name: Inevitable Death
features.description: Mark a Stress to spotlight 1d4 allies. Attacks they make while spotlighted in this way deal half damage.

features.name: Rampage
features.description: Countdown (Loop 1d6). When the Hunter is in the spotlight for the first time, activate the countdown. When it triggers, move the Hunter in a straight line to a point within Far range and make an attack against all targets in their path. Targets the Hunter succeeds against take 2d8+2 physical damage.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 2 > Royal Advisor
entries.name: Royal Advisor

fields.description: A high-ranking courtier with the ear of the local nobility.

features.name: Devastating Retort
features.description: A PC who rolls less than 17 on an action roll targeting the Advisor must mark a Stress.

features.name: Bend Ears
features.description: Mark a Stress to influence an NPC within Melee range with whispered words. That target’s opinion on one matter shifts toward the Advisor’s preference unless it is in direct opposition to the target’s motives.

features.name: Scapegoat
features.description: Spend a Fear to convince a crowd or notable individual that one person or group is responsible for some problem facing the target. The target becomes hostile to the scapegoat until convinced of their innocence with a successful Presence Roll (17).

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 2 > Secret-Keeper
entries.name: Secret-Keeper

fields.description: A clandestine leader with a direct channel to the Fallen Gods.
fields.motives: Amass great power, plot, take command

features.name: Seize Your Moment
features.description: Spend 2 Fear to spotlight 1d4 allies. Attacks they make while spotlighted in this way deal half damage

features.name: Our Master’s Will
features.description: When you spotlight an ally within Far range, mark a Stress to gain a Fear.

features.name: Summoning Ritual
features.description: Countdown (6). When the Secret-Keeper is in the spotlight for the first time, activate the countdown. When they mark HP, tick down this countdown by the number of HP marked. When it triggers, summon a Minor Demon who appears at Close range.

features.name: Fallen Hounds
features.description: Once per scene, when the Secret-Keeper marks 2 or more HP, you can mark a Stress to summon a Demonic Hound Pack, which appears at Close range and is immediately spotlighted.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 2 > Shark
entries.name: Shark

fields.description: A large aquatic predator, always on the move.
fields.motives: Find the blood, isolate prey, target the weak

features.name: Terrifying
features.description: When the Shark makes a successful attack, all PCs within Far range lose a Hope and you gain a Fear.

features.name: Rending Bite
features.description: When the Shark makes a successful attack, the target must mark an Armor Slot without receiving its benefits (they can still use armor to reduce the damage). If they can’t mark an Armor Slot, they must mark an additional HP.

features.name: Blood in the Water
features.description: When a creature within Close range of the Shark marks HP from another creature’s attack, you can mark a Stress to immediately spotlight the Shark, moving them into Melee range of the target and making a standard attack.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 2 > Siren
entries.name: Siren

fields.description: A half-fi sh person with shimmering scales and an irresistible voice.
fields.motives: Consume, lure prey, subdue with song

features.name: Captive Audience
features.description: If the Siren makes a standard attack against a target Entranced by their song, the attack deals 2d10+1 damage instead of their standard damage.

features.name: Enchanting Song
features.description: Spend a Fear to sing a song that aff ects all targets within Close range. Targets must succeed on an Instinct Reaction Roll or become Entranced until they mark 2 Stress. Other Sirens within Close range of the target can mark

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 2 > Spectral Archer
entries.name: Spectral Archer

fields.description: A ghostly fighter with an ethereal bow, unable to move on while their charge is vulnerable.
fields.motives: Move through solid objects, stay out of the fray, rehash old battles

features.name: Ghost
features.description: The Archer has resistance to physical damage. Mark a Stress to move up to Close range through solid objects.

features.name: Pick Your Target
features.description: Spend a Fear to make an attack within Far range against a PC who is within Very Close range of at least two other PCs. On a success, the target takes 2d8+12 physical damage.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 2 > Spectral Captain
entries.name: Spectral Captain

fields.description: A ghostly commander leading their troops beyond death.
fields.motives: Move through solid objects, rally troops, rehash old battles

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 2 > Spectral Guardian
entries.name: Spectral Guardian

fields.description: A ghostly fighter with spears and swords, anchored by duty.
fields.motives: Move through solid objects, protect treasure, rehash old battles

features.name: Ghost
features.description: The Guardian has resistance to physical damage. Mark a Stress to move up to Close range through solid objects.

features.name: Grave Blade
features.description: Spend a Fear to make an attack against a target within Very Close range. On a success, deal 2d10+6 physical damage and the target must mark a Stress.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 2 > Spy
entries.name: Spy

fields.description: A skilled espionage agent with a knack for being in the right place to overhear secrets.
fields.motives: Cut and run, disguise appearance, eavesdrop

features.name: Gathering Secrets
features.description: Spend a Fear to describe how the Spy knows a secret about a PC in the scene.

features.name: Fly on the Wall
features.description: When a PC or group is discussing something sensitive, you can mark a Stress to reveal that the Spy is present in the scene, observing them. If the Spy escapes the scene to report their findings, you gain 1d4 Fear.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 2 > Stonewraith
entries.name: Stonewraith

fields.description: A prowling hunter, like a slinking mountain lion, with a slate-gray stone body.

features.name: Stonestrider
features.description: The Stonewraith can move through stone and earth as easily as air. While within stone or earth, they are Hidden and immune to all damage.

features.name: Rocky Ambush
features.description: While Hidden, mark a Stress to leap into Melee range with a target within Very Close range. The target must succeed on an Agility or Instinct Reaction Roll (15) or take 2d8 physical damage and become temporarily Restrained.

features.name: Avalanche Roar
features.description: Spend a Fear to roar while within a cave and cause a cave-in. All targets within Close range must succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll (14) or take 2d10 physical damage. The rubble can be cleared with a Progress Countdown (8).

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When the Stonewraith makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 2 > War Wizard
entries.name: War Wizard

fields.description: A battle-hardened mage trained in destructive magic.
fields.motives: Develop new spells, seek power, shatter formations

features.name: Battle Teleport
features.description: Before or after making a standard attack, you can mark a Stress to teleport to a location within Far range.

features.name: Refresh Warding Sphere
features.description: Mark a Stress to refresh the Wizard’s “Warding Sphere” reaction.

features.name: Eruption
features.description: Spend a Fear and choose a point within Far range. A Very Close area around that point erupts into impassable terrain. All targets within that area must make an Agility Reaction Roll (14). Targets who fail take 2d10 physical damage and are thrown out of the area. Targets who succeed take half damage and aren’t moved.

features.name: Arcane Artillery
features.description: Spend a Fear to unleash a precise hail of magical blasts. All targets in the scene must make an Agility Reaction Roll. Targets who fail take 2d12 magic damage. Targets who succeed take half damage.

features.name: Warding Sphere
features.description: When the Wizard takes damage from an attack within Close range, deal 2d6 magic damage to the attacker. This reaction can’t be used again until the Wizard refreshes it with their “Refresh Warding Sphere” action.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 3 > Adult Flickerfly
entries.name: Adult Flickerfly

fields.description: A winged insect the size of a large house with iridescent scales and wings that move too fast to track.
fields.motives: Collect shiny things, hunt, nest, swoop

features.name: Relentless (4)
features.description: The Flickerfly can be spotlighted up to four times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Never Misses
features.description: When the Flickerfly makes an attack, the target’s Evasion is halved against the attack.

features.name: Deadly Flight
features.description: While flying, the Flickerfly can move up to Far range instead of Close range before taking an action.

features.name: Whirlwind
features.description: Spend a Fear to whirl, making an attack against all targets within Very Close range. Targets the Flickerfly succeeds against take 3d8 direct physical damage.

features.name: Mind Dance
features.description: Mark a Stress to create a magically dazzling display that grapples the minds of nearby foes. All targets within Close range must make an Instinct Reaction Roll. For each target who failed, you gain a Fear and the Flickerfly learns one of the target’s fears.

features.name: Hallucinatory Breath
features.description: Countdown (Loop 1d6).

features.name: Uncanny Reflexes
features.description: When the Flickerfly takes damage from an attack within Close range, you can mark a Stress to take half damage.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 3 > Demon Of Avarice
entries.name: Demon Of Avarice

fields.description: A regal cloaked monstrosity with circular horns adorned with treasure.

features.name: Money Talks
features.description: Attacks against the Demon are made with disadvantage unless the attacker spends a handful of gold. This Demon starts with a number of handfuls equal to the number of PCs. When a target marks HP from the Demon’s standard attack, they can spend a handful of gold instead of marking HP (1 handful per HP). Add a handful of gold to the Demon for each handful of gold spent by PCs on this feature.

features.name: Numbers Must Go Up
features.description: Add a bonus to the Demon’s attack rolls equal to the number of handfuls of gold they have.

features.name: Money Is Time
features.description: Spend 3 handfuls of gold (or a Fear) to spotlight 1d4+1 allies.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 3 > Demon Of Despair
entries.name: Demon Of Despair

fields.description: A cloaked demon-creature with long limbs, seeping shadows.
fields.motives: Make fear contagious, stick to the shadows, undermine resolve

features.name: Depths of Despair
features.description: The Demon deals double damage to PCs with 0 Hope.

features.name: Your Struggle Is Pointless
features.description: Spend a Fear to weigh down the spirits of all PCs within Far range. All targets aff ected replace their Hope Die with a d8 until they roll a success with Hope or their next rest.

features.name: Your Friends Will Fail You
features.description: When a PC fails with Fear, you can mark a Stress to cause all other PCs within Close range to lose a Hope.

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When the Demon makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 3 > Demon Of Jealousy
entries.name: Demon Of Jealousy

fields.description: A fickle creature of spindly limbs and insatiable desires.
fields.motives: Join in on others’ success, take what belongs to others, hold grudges

features.name: Unprotected Mind
features.description: The Demon’s standard attack deals direct damage.

features.name: My Turn
features.description: When the Demon marks HP from an attack, spend a number of Fear equal to the HP marked by the Demon to cause the attacker to mark the same number of HP.

features.name: Rivalry
features.description: When a creature within Close range takes damage from a diff erent adversary, you can mark a Stress to add a d4 to the damage roll.

features.name: What’s Yours Is Mine
features.description: When a PC takes Severe damage within Very Close range of the Demon, you can spend a Fear to cause the target to make a Finesse Reaction Roll. On a failure, the Demon seizes one item or consumable of their choice from the target’s inventory.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 3 > Demon Of Wrath
entries.name: Demon Of Wrath

fields.description: A hulking demon with boulder-sized fists, driven by endless rage.
fields.motives: Fuel anger, impress rivals, wreak havoc

features.name: Anger Unrelenting
features.description: The Demon’s attacks deal direct damage.

features.name: Battle Lust
features.description: Spend a Fear to boil the blood of all PCs within Far range. They use a d20 as their Fear Die until the end of the scene.

features.name: Retaliation
features.description: When the Demon takes damage from an attack within Close range, you can mark a Stress to make a standard attack against the attacker.

features.name: Blood and Souls
features.description: Countdown (Loop 6). Activate the first time an attack is made within sight of the Demon. It ticks down when a PC takes a violent action. When it triggers, summon 1d4 Minor Demons, who appear at Close range.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 3 > Dire Bat
entries.name: Dire Bat

fields.description: A wide-winged pet endlessly loyal to their vampire owner.

features.name: Flying
features.description: While flying, the Bat gains a + 3 bonus to their Difficulty.

features.name: Screech
features.description: Mark a Stress to send a high-pitch screech out toward all targets in front of the Bat within Far range. Those targets must mark 1d4 Stress.

features.name: Guardian
features.description: When an allied Vampire marks HP, you can mark a Stress to fly into Melee range of the attacker and make an attack with advantage against them. On a success, deal 2d6+2 physical damage.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 3 > Dryad
entries.name: Dryad

fields.description: A nature spirit in the form of a humanoid tree.
fields.motives: Command, cultivate, drive out, preserve the forest

features.name: Bramble Patch
features.description: Mark a Stress to target a point within Far range. Create a patch of thorns that covers an area within Close range of that point. All targets within that area take 2d6+2 physical damage when they act. A target must succeed on a Finesse Roll or deal more than 20 damage to the Dryad with an attack to leave the area.

features.name: Grow Saplings
features.description: Spend a Fear to grow three Treant Sapling Minions, who appear at Close range and immediately take the spotlight.

features.name: We Are All One
features.description: When an ally dies within Close range, you can spend a Fear to clear 2 HP and 2 Stress as the fallen ally’s life force is returned to the forest.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 3 > Elemental Spark
entries.name: Elemental Spark

fields.description: A blazing mote of elemental fire.

features.name: Minion (9)
features.description: The Elemental is defeated when they take any damage. For every 9 damage a PC deals to the Elemental, defeat an additional Minion within range the attack would succeed against.

features.name: Group Attack
features.description: Spend a Fear to choose a target and spotlight all Elemental Sparks within Close range of them. Those Minions move into Melee range of the target and make one shared attack roll. On a success, they deal 5 physical damage each. Combine this damage.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 3 > Greater Earth Elemental
entries.name: Greater Earth Elemental

fields.description: A living landslide of boulders and dust, as large as a house.

features.name: Slow
features.description: When you spotlight the Elemental and they don’t have a token on their stat block, they can’t act yet. Place a token on their stat block and describe what they’re preparing to do. When you spotlight the Elemental and they have a token on their stat block, clear the token and they can act.

features.name: Crushing Blows
features.description: When the Elemental makes a successful attack, the target must mark an Armor Slot without receiving its benefits (they can still use armor to reduce the damage). If they can’t mark an Armor Slot, they must mark an additional HP.

features.name: Immovable Object
features.description: An attack that would move the Elemental moves them two fewer ranges (for example, Fa becomes Very Close). When the Elemental takes physical damage, reduce it by 7.

features.name: Rockslide
features.description: Mark a Stress to create a rockslide that buries the land in front of Elemental within Close range with rockfall. All targets in this area must make an Agility Reaction Roll (19). Targets who fail take 2d12+5 physical damage and become Vulnerable until their next roll with Hope. Targets who succeed take half damage.

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When the Elemental makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 3 > Greater Water Elemental
entries.name: Greater Water Elemental

fields.description: A huge living wave that crashes down upon enemies.

features.name: Water Jet
features.description: Mark a Stress to attack a target within Very Close range. On a success, deal 2d4+7 physical damage and the target’s next action has disadvantage. On a failure, the target must mark a Stress.

features.name: Drowning Embrace
features.description: Spend a Fear to make an attack against all targets within Very Close range. Targets the Elemental succeeds against become Restrained and Vulnerable as they begin drowning. A target can break free, ending both conditions, with a successful Strength or Instinct Roll.

features.name: High Tide
features.description: When the Elemental makes a successful standard attack, you can mark a Stress to knock the target back to Close range.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 3 > Huge Green Ooze
entries.name: Huge Green Ooze

fields.description: A translucent green mound of acid taller than most humans.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 3 > Hydra
entries.name: Hydra

fields.description: A quadrupedal scaled beast with multiple long-necked heads, each filled with menacing fangs.

features.name: Many-Headed Menace
features.description: The Hydra begins with three heads and can have up to five. When the Hydra takes Major or greater damage, they lose a head.

features.name: Relentless (X)
features.description: The Hydra can be spotlighted X times per GM turn, where X is the Hydra’s number of heads. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Regeneration
features.description: If the Hydra has any marked HP, spend a Fear to clear a HP and grow two heads.

features.name: Terrifying Chorus
features.description: All PCs within Far range lose 2 Hope.

features.name: Magical Weakness
features.description: When the Hydra takes magic damage, they become Dazed until the next roll with Fear. While Dazed, they can’t use their Regeneration action but are immune to magic damage.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 3 > Monarch
entries.name: Monarch

fields.description: The sovereign ruler of a nation, wreathed in the privilege of tradition and wielding unmatched power in their domain.
fields.motives: Control vassals, destroy rivals, forge a legacy

features.name: Execute Them!
features.description: Spend a Fear per PC in the party to have the group condemned for crimes real or imagined. A PC who succeeds on a Presence Roll can demand trial by combat or another special form of trial.

features.name: Crownsguard
features.description: Once per scene, mark a Stress to summon six Tier 3 Minions, who appear at Close range to enforce the Monarch’s will.

features.name: Casus Belli
features.description: Long-Term Countdown (8). Spend a Fear to activate after the Monarch’s desire for war is first revealed. When it triggers, the Monarch has a reason to rally the nation to war and the support to act on that reason. You gain 1d4 Fear.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 3 > Stag Knight
entries.name: Stag Knight

fields.description: A knight with huge, majestic antlers wearing armor made of dangerous thorns.
fields.motives: Isolate, maneuver, protect the forest, weed the unwelcome

features.name: From Above
features.description: When the Knight succeeds on a standard attack from above a target, they deal 3d12+3 physical damage instead of their standard damage.

features.name: Blade of the Forest
features.description: Spend a Fear to make an attack against all targets within Very Close range. Targets the Knight succeeds against take physical damage equal to 3d4 + the target’s Major threshold.

features.name: Thorny Armor
features.description: When the Knight takes damage from an attack within Melee range, you can mark a Stress to deal 1d10+5 physical damage to the attacker.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 3 > Oak Treant
entries.name: Oak Treant

fields.description: A sturdy animate old-growth tree.
fields.motives: Hide in plain sight, preserve the forest, root down, swing branches

features.name: Just a Tree
features.description: Before they make their first attack in a fight or after they become Hidden, the Treant is indistinguishable from other trees until they next act or a PC succeeds on an Instinct Roll to identify them.

features.name: Seed Barrage
features.description: Mark a Stress and make an attack against up to three targets within Close range, pummeling them with giant acorns. Targets the Treant succeeds against take 2d10+5 physical damage.

features.name: Take Root
features.description: Mark a Stress to Root the Treant in place. The Treant is Restrained while Rooted, and can end this eff ect instead of moving while they are spotlighted. While Rooted, the Treant has resistance to physical damage.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 3 > Head Vampire
entries.name: Head Vampire

fields.description: A captivating undead dressed in aristocratic fi nery.
fields.motives: Create thralls, charm, command, fly, intimidate

features.name: Terrifying
features.description: When the Vampire makes a successful attack, all PCs within Far range lose a Hope and you gain a Fear.

features.name: Look into My Eyes
features.description: A creature who moves into Melee range of the Vampire must make an Instinct Reaction Roll. On a failure, you gain 1d4 Fear.

features.name: Feed on Followers
features.description: When the Vampire is within Melee range of an ally, they can cause the ally to mark a HP. The Vampire then clears a HP.

features.name: The Hunt Is On
features.description: Spend 2 Fear to summon 1d4 Vampires, who appear at Far range and immediately take the spotlight.

features.name: Lifesuck
features.description: When the Vampire is spotlighted, roll a d8. On a result of 6 or higher, all targets within Very Close range must mark a HP.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 3 > Treant Sapling
entries.name: Treant Sapling

fields.description: A small, sentient tree sapling.
fields.motives: Blend in, preserve the forest, pummel, surround

features.name: Minion (6)
features.description: The Sapling is defeated when they take any damage. For every 6 damage a PC deals to the Sapling, defeat an additional Minion within range the attack would succeed against.

features.name: Group Attack
features.description: Spend a Fear to choose a target and spotlight all Treant Saplings within Close range of them. Those Minions move into Melee range of the target and make one shared attack roll. On a success, they deal 8 physical damage each. Combine this damage.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 3 > Vampire
entries.name: Vampire

fields.description: An intelligent undead with blood-stained lips and a predator’s smile.

features.name: Draining Bite
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Melee range. On a success, deal 5d4 physical damage. A target who marks HP from this attack loses a Hope and must mark a Stress. The Vampire then clears a HP.

features.name: Mistform
features.description: When the Vampire takes physical damage, you can spend a Fear to take half damage.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 3 > Vault Guardian Gaoler
entries.name: Vault Guardian Gaoler

fields.description: A boxy, dust-covered construct with thick metallic swinging doors on their torso.

features.name: Blocking Shield
features.description: Creatures within Melee range of the Gaoler have disadvantage on attack rolls against them. Creatures trapped inside the Gaoler are immune to this feature.

features.name: Lock Up
features.description: Mark a Stress to make an attack against a target within Very Close range. On a success, the target is Restrained within the Gaoler until freed with a successful Strength Roll (18). While Restrained, the target can only attack the Gaoler.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 3 > Vault Guardian Sentinel
entries.name: Vault Guardian Sentinel

fields.description: A dust-covered golden construct with boxy limbs and a huge mace for a hand.
fields.motives: Destroy at any cost, expunge, protect

features.name: Kinetic Slam
features.description: Targets who take damage from the Sentinel’s standard attack are knocked back to Very Close range.

features.name: Box In
features.description: Mark a Stress to choose a target within Very Close range to focus on. That target has disadvantage on attack rolls when they’re within Very Close range of the Sentinel. The Sentinel can only focus on one target at a time.

features.name: Mana Bolt
features.description: Spend a Fear to lob explosive magic at a point within Far range. All targets within Very Close range of that point must make an Agility Reaction Roll. Targets who fail take 2d8+20 magic damage and are knocked back to Close range. Targets who succeed take half damage and aren’t knocked back.

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When the Sentinel makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 3 > Vault Guardian Turret
entries.name: Vault Guardian Turret

fields.description: A massive living turret with reinforced armor and twelve pistondriven mechanical legs.
fields.motives: Concentrate fire, lock down, mark, protect

features.name: Slow Firing
features.description: When you spotlight the Turret and they don’t have a token on their stat block, they can’t make a standard attack. Place a token on their stat block and describe what they’re preparing to do. When you spotlight the Turret and they have a token on their stat block, clear the token and they can attack.

features.name: Mark Target
features.description: Spend a Fear to Mark a target within Far range until the Turret is destroyed or the Marked target becomes Hidden. While the target is Marked, their Evasion is halved.

features.name: Concentrate Fire
features.description: When another adversary deals damage to a target within Far range of the Turret, you can mark a Stress to add the Turret’s standard attack damage to the damage roll.

features.name: Detonation
features.description: When the Turret is destroyed, they explode. All targets within Close range must make an Agility Reaction Roll. Targets who fail take 3d20 physical damage. Targets who succeed take half damage.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 3 > Young Ice Dragon
entries.name: Young Ice Dragon

fields.description: A glacier-blue dragon with four powerful limbs and frost-tinged wings.
fields.motives: Avalanche, defend lair, fly, freeze, defend what is mine, maul

features.name: Relentless (3)
features.description: The Dragon can be spotlighted up to three times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Rend and Crush
features.description: If a target damaged by the Dragon doesn’t mark an Armor Slot to reduce the damage, they must mark a Stress.

features.name: No Hope
features.description: When a PC rolls with Fear while within Far range of the Dragon, they lose a Hope.

features.name: Blizzard Breath
features.description: Spend 2 Fear to release an icy whorl in front of the Dragon within Close range. All targets in this area must make an Agility Reaction Roll. Targets who fail take 4d6+5 magic damage and are Restrained by ice until they break free with a successful Strength Roll. Targets who succeed must mark 2 Stress or take half damage.

features.name: Avalanche
features.description: Spend a Fear to have the Dragon unleash a huge downfall of snow and ice, covering all other creatures within Far range. All targets within this area must succeed on an Instinct Reaction Roll or be buried in snow and rocks, becoming Vulnerable until they dig themselves out from the debris. For each PC that fails the reaction roll, you gain a Fear.

features.name: Frozen Scales
features.description: When a creature makes a successful attack against the Dragon from within Very Close range, they must mark a Stress and become Chilled until their next rest or they clear a Stress. While they are Chilled, they have disadvantage on attack rolls.

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When the Dragon makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 4 > Arch-Necromancer
entries.name: Arch-Necromancer

fields.description: A decaying mage adorned in dark, tattered robes.
fields.motives: Corrupt, decay, flee to fight another day, resurrect

features.name: Dance of Death
features.description: Mark a Stress to spotlight 1d4 allies. Attacks they make while spotlighted in this way deal half damage, or full damage if you spend a Fear.

features.name: Beam of Decay
features.description: Mark 2 Stress to cause all targets within Far range to make a Strength Reaction Roll. Targets who fail take 2d20+12 magic damage and you gain a Fear. Targets who succeed take half damage. A target who marks 2 or more HP must also mark 2 Stress and becomes Vulnerable until they roll with Hope.

features.name: Open the Gates of Death
features.description: Spend a Fear to summon a Zombie Legion, which appears at Close range and immediately takes the spotlight.

features.name: Not Today, My Dears
features.description: When the Necromancer has marked 7 or more of their HP, you can spend a Fear to have them teleport away to a safe location to recover. A PC who succeeds on an Instinct Roll can trace the teleportation magic to their destination.

features.name: Your Life Is Mine
features.description: Countdown (Loop 2d6). When the Necromancer has marked 6 or more of their HP, activate the countdown. When it triggers, deal 2d10+6 direct magic damage to a target within Close range. The Necromancer then clears a number of Stress or HP equal to the number of HP marked by the target from this attack.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 4 > Fallen Shock Troop
entries.name: Fallen Shock Troop

fields.description: A cursed soul bound to the Fallen’s will.

features.name: Minion (12)
features.description: The Shock Troop is defeated when they take any damage. For every 12 damage a PC deals to the Shock Troop, defeat an additional Minion within range the attack would succeed against.

features.name: Aura of Doom
features.description: When a PC marks HP from an attack by the Shock Troop, they lose a Hope.

features.name: Group Attack
features.description: Spend a Fear to choose a target and spotlight all Fallen Shock Troops within Close range of them. Those Minions move into Melee range of the target and make one shared attack roll. On a success, they deal 12 physical damage each. Combine this damage.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 4 > Fallen Sorcerer
entries.name: Fallen Sorcerer

fields.description: A powerful mage bound by the bargains they made in life.

features.name: Conflagration
features.description: Spend a Fear to unleash an all-consuming firestorm and make an attack against all targets within Close range. Targets the Sorcerer succeeds against take 2d10+6 direct magic damage.

features.name: Nightmare Tableau
features.description: Mark a Stress to trap a target within Far range in a powerful illusion of their worst fears. While trapped, the target is Restrained and Vulnerable until they break free, ending both conditions, with a successful Instinct Roll.

features.name: Slippery
features.description: When the Sorcerer takes damage from an attack, they can teleport up to Far range.

features.name: Shackles of Guilt
features.description: Countdown (Loop 2d6). When the Sorcerer is in the spotlight for the first time, activate the countdown. When it triggers, all targets within Far range become Vulnerable and must mark a Stress as they relive their greatest regrets. A target can break free from their regret with a successful Presence or Strength Roll. When a PC fails to break free, they lose a Hope.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 4 > Fallen Warlord: Realm-Breaker
entries.name: Fallen Warlord: Realm-Breaker

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 4 > Fallen Warlord: Undefeated Champion
entries.name: Fallen Warlord: Undefeated Champion

fields.description: That which only the most feared have a chance to fear.
fields.motives: Dispatch merciless death, punish the defiant, secure victory at any cost

features.name: Relentless (3)
features.description: The Undefeated Champion can be spotlighted up to three times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Faltering Armor
features.description: When the Undefeated Champion takes damage, reduce it by 1d10.

features.name: Shattering Strike
features.description: Mark a Stress to make a standard attack against all targets within Very Close range. PCs the Champion succeeds against lose a number of Hope equal to the HP they marked from this attack.

features.name: Endless Legions
features.description: Spend a Fear to summon a number of Fallen Shock Troops equal to twice the number of PCs. The Shock Troops appear at Far range.

features.name: Circle of Defilement
features.description: Countdown (1d8). When the Undefeated Champion is in the spotlight for the first time, activate the countdown. When it triggers, activate a magical circle covering an area within Far range of the Champion. A target within that area is Vulnerable until they leave the circle The circle can be removed by dealing Severe damage to the Undefeated Champion.

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When the Undefeated Champion makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.

features.name: Doombringer
features.description: When a target marks HP from an attack by the Undefeated Champion, all PCs within Far range of the target lose a Hope.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 4 > Hallowed Archer
entries.name: Hallowed Archer

fields.description: Spirit soldiers with sanctified bows.

features.name: Punish the Guilty
features.description: The Archer deals double damage to targets marked Guilty by a High Seraph.

features.name: Divine Volley
features.description: Mark a Stress to make a standard attack against up to three targets.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 4 > Hallowed Soldier
entries.name: Hallowed Soldier

fields.description: Souls of the faithful, lifted up with divine weaponry.

features.name: Minion (13)
features.description: The Soldier is defeated when they take any damage. For every 13 damage a PC deals to the Soldier, defeat an additional Minion within range the attack would succeed against.

features.name: Divine Flight
features.description: While the Soldier is flying, spend a Fear to move up to Far range instead of Close range before taking an action.

features.name: Group Attack
features.description: Spend a Fear to choose a target and spotlight all Hallowed Soldiers within Close range of them. Those Minions move into Melee range of the target and make one shared attack roll. On a success, they deal 10 physical damage each. Combine this damage.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 4 > High Seraph
entries.name: High Seraph

fields.description: A divine champion, head of a hallowed host of warriors who enforce their god’s will.
fields.motives: Enforce dogma, fly, pronounce judgment, smite

features.name: Relentless (3)
features.description: The Seraph can be spotlighted up to three times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Divine Flight
features.description: While the Seraph is flying, spend a Fear to move up to Far range instead of Close range before taking an action.

features.name: Judgment
features.description: Spend a Fear to make a target Guilty in the eyes of the Seraph’s god until the Seraph is defeated. While Guilty, the target doesn’t gain Hope on a result with Hope. When the Seraph succeeds on a standard attack against a Guilty target, they deal Severe damage instead of their standard damage. The Seraph can only mark one target at a time.

features.name: God Rays
features.description: Mark a Stress to reflect a sliver of divinity as a searing beam of light that hits up to twenty targets within Very Far range. Targets must make a Presence Reaction Roll, with disadvantage if they are marked Guilty. Targets who fail take 4d6+12 magic damage. Targets who succeed take half damage.

features.name: We Are One
features.description: Once per scene, spend a Fear to spotlight all other adversaries within Far range. Attacks they make while spotlighted in this way deal half damage.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 4 > Kraken
entries.name: Kraken

fields.description: A legendary beast of the sea, bigger than the largest galleon, with sucker-laden tentacles and a terrifying maw.

features.name: Relentless (3)
features.description: The Kraken can be spotlighted up to three times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Many Tentacles
features.description: While the Kraken has 7 or fewer marked HP, they can make their standard attack against two targets within range.

features.name: Grapple and Drown
features.description: Make an attack roll against a target within Close range. On a success, mark a Stress to grab them with a tentacle and drag them beneath the water. The target is Restrained and Vulnerable until they break free with a successful Strength Roll or the Kraken takes Major or greater damage. While Restrained and Vulnerable in this way, a target must mark a Stress when they make an action roll.

features.name: Boiling Blast
features.description: Spend a Fear to spew a line of boiling water at any number of targets in a line up to Far range. All targets must succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll or take 4d6+9 physical damage. If a target marks an Armor Slot to reduce the damage, they must also mark a Stress.

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When the Kraken makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 4 > Oracle Of Doom
entries.name: Oracle Of Doom

fields.description: A towering immortal and incarnation of fate, cursed to only see bad outcomes.
fields.motives: Change environment, condemn, dishearten, toss aside

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 4 > Outer Realms Abomination
entries.name: Outer Realms Abomination

fields.description: A chaotic mockery of life, constantly in flux.

features.name: Chaotic Form
features.description: When the Abomination attacks, roll 2d4 and use the result as their attack modifier.

features.name: Disorienting Presence
features.description: When a target takes damage from the Abomination, they must make an Instinct Reaction Roll. On a failure, they gain disadvantage on their next action roll and you gain a Fear.

features.name: Reality Quake
features.description: Spend a Fear to rattle the edges of reality within Far range of the Abomination. All targets within that area must succeed on a Knowledge Reaction Roll or become Unstuck from reality until the end of the scene. When an Unstuck target spends Hope or marks Armor Slots, HP, or Stress, they must double the amount spent or marked.

features.name: Unreal Form
features.description: When the Abomination takes damage, reduce it by 1d20. If the Abomination marks 1 or fewer Hit Points from a successful attack against them, you gain a Fear.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 4 > Outer Realms Corrupter
entries.name: Outer Realms Corrupter

fields.description: A shifting, formless mass seemingly made of chromatic light.

features.name: Will-Shattering Touch
features.description: When a PC takes damage from the Corrupter, they lose a Hope.

features.name: Disgorge Reality Flotsam
features.description: Mark a Stress to spew partially digested portions of consumed realities at all targets within Close range. Targets must succeed on a Knowledge Reaction Roll or mark 2 Stress.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 4 > Outer Realms Thrall
entries.name: Outer Realms Thrall

fields.description: A vaguely humanoid form stripped of memory and identity.
fields.attack: +3 | Claws and Teeth: Very Close | 11 phy

features.name: Minion (13)
features.description: The Thrall is defeated when they take any damage. For every 13 damage a PC deals to the Thrall, defeat an additional Minion within range the attack would succeed against.

features.name: Group Attack
features.description: Spend a Fear to choose a target and spotlight all Outer Realm Thralls within Close range of them. Those Minions move into Melee range of the target and make one shared attack roll. On a success, they deal 11 physical damage each. Combine this damage.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 4 > Volcanic Dragon: Obsidian Predator
entries.name: Volcanic Dragon: Obsidian Predator

fields.description: A massive winged creature with obsidian scales and impossibly sharp claws.
fields.motives: Defend lair, dive-bomb, fly, hunt, intimidate

features.name: Relentless (2)
features.description: The Obsidian Predator can be spotlighted up to two times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Flying
features.description: While flying, the Obsidian Predator gains a +3 bonus to their Difficulty.

features.name: Obsidian Scales
features.description: The Obsidian Predator is resistant to physical damage.

features.name: Avalanche Tail
features.description: Mark a Stress to make an attack against all targets within Close range. Targets the Obsidian Predator succeeds against take 4d6+4 physical damage and are knocked back to Far range and Vulnerable until their next roll with Hope.

features.name: Dive-Bomb
features.description: If the Obsidian Predator is flying, mark a Stress to choose a point within Far range. Move to that point and make an attack against all targets within Very Close range Targets the Obsidian Predator succeeds against take 2d10+6 physical damage and must mark a Stress and lose a Hope.

features.name: Erupting Rage (Phase Change)
features.description: When the Obsidian Predator marks their last HP, replace them with the Molten Scourge and immediately spotlight them.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 4 > Volcanic Dragon: Molten Scourge
entries.name: Volcanic Dragon: Molten Scourge

fields.description: Enraged by their wounds, the dragon bursts into molten lava.
fields.motives: Douse with lava, incinerate, repel Invaders, reposition

features.name: Relentless (3)
features.description: The Molten Scourge can be spotlighted up to three times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Cracked Scales
features.description: When the Molten Scourge takes damage, roll a number of d6s equal to HP marked. For each result of 4 or higher, you gain a Fear.

features.name: Shattering Might
features.description: Mark a Stress to make an attack against a target within Very Close range. On a success, the target takes 4d8+1 physical damage, loses a Hope, and is knocked back to Close range. The Molten Scourge clears a Stress.

features.name: Eruption
features.description: Spend a Fear to erupt lava from beneath the Molten Scourge’s scales, fi lling the area within Very Close range with molten lava. All targets in that area must succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll or take 4d6+6 physical damage and be knocked back to Close range. This area remains lava. When a creature other than the Molten Scourge enters that area or acts while inside of it, they must mark 6 HP.

features.name: Volcanic Breath
features.description: When the Molten Scourge takes Major damage, roll a d10. On a result of 8 or higher, the Molten Scourge breathes a flow of lava in front of them within Far range. All targets in that area must make an Agility Reaction Roll. Targets who fail take 2d10+4 physical damage, mark 1d4 Stress, and are Vulnerable until they clear a Stress. Targets who succeed take half damage and must mark a Stress.

features.name: Lava Splash
features.description: When the Molten Scourge takes Severe damage from an attack within Very Close range, molten blood gushes from the wound and deals 2d10+4 direct physical damage to the attacker.

features.name: Ashen Vengeance (Phase Change)
features.description: When the Molten Scourge marks their last HP, replace them with the Ashen Tyrant and immediately spotlight them.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 4 > Volcanic Dragon: Ashen Tyrant
entries.name: Volcanic Dragon: Ashen Tyrant

fields.description: No enemy has ever had the insolence to wound the dragon so. As the lava settles, it’s ground to ash like the dragon’s past foes.
fields.motives: Choke, fly, intimidate, kill or be killed

features.name: Relentless (4)
features.description: The Ashen Tyrant can be spotlighted up to four times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Cornered
features.description: Mark a Stress instead of spending a Fear to spotlight the Ashen Tyrant.

features.name: Injured Wings
features.description: While flying, the Ashen Tyrant gains a +1 bonus to their Difficulty.

features.name: Ashes to Ashes
features.description: When a PC rolls a failure while within Close range of the Ashen Tyrant, they lose a Hope and you gain a Fear. If the PC can’t lose a Hope, they must mark a HP.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 4 > Perfected Zombie
entries.name: Perfected Zombie

fields.description: A towering, muscular zombie with magically infused strength and skill.

features.name: Terrifying
features.description: When the Zombie makes a successful attack, all PCs within Far range lose a Hope and you gain a Fear.

features.name: Fearsome Presence
features.description: PCs can’t spend Hope to use features against the Zombie.

features.name: Perfect Strike
features.description: Mark a Stress to make a standard attack against all targets within Very Close range. Targets the Zombie succeeds against are Vulnerable until their next rest.

features.name: Skilled Opportunist
features.description: When another adversary deals damage to a target within Very Close range of the Zombie, you can spend a Fear to add the Zombie’s standard attack damage to the damage roll.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 4 > Zombie Legion
entries.name: Zombie Legion

fields.description: A large pack of undead, still powerful despite their rotting flesh.

features.name: Horde 2d6+5
features.description: When the Legion has marked half or more of their HP, their standard attack deals 2d6+5 physical damage instead.

features.name: Unyielding
features.description: The Legion has resistance to physical damage.

features.name: Relentless (2)
features.description: The Legion can be spotlighted up to two times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Overwhelm
features.description: When the Legion takes Minor damage from an attack within Melee range, you can mark a Stress to make a standard attack with advantage against the attacker.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 1 > Abandoned Grove
entries.name: Abandoned Grove

fields.description: A former druidic grove lying fallow and fully reclaimed by nature.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 1 > Ambushed
entries.name: Ambushed

fields.description: An ambush is set to catch an unsuspecting party off -guard.

features.name: Relative Strength
features.description: The Difficulty of this environment equals that of the adversary with the highest Difficulty. Who cues the ambush? What makes it clear they’re in charge?

features.name: Surprise!
features.description: The ambushers reveal themselves to the party, you gain 2 Fear, and the spotlight immediately shifts to one of the ambushing adversaries.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 1 > Ambushers
entries.name: Ambushers

fields.description: An ambush is set by the PCs to catch unsuspecting adversaries off -guard.

features.name: Relative Strength
features.description: The Difficulty of this environment equals that of the adversary with the highest Difficulty.

features.name: Where Did They Come From?
features.description: When a PC starts the ambush on unsuspecting adversaries, you lose 2 Fear and the first attack roll a PC makes has advantage.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 1 > Bustling Marketplace
entries.name: Bustling Marketplace

fields.description: The economic heart of the settlement, with local artisans, traveling merchants, and patrons across social classes.

features.name: Tip the Scales
features.description: PCs can gain advantage on a Presence Roll by off ering a handful of gold as part of the interaction.

features.name: Unexpected Find
features.description: Reveal to the PCs that one of the merchants has something they want or need, such as food from their home, a rare book, magical components, a dubious treasure map, or a magical key.

features.name: Sticky Fingers
features.description: A thief tries to steal something from a PC.

features.name: Crowd Closes In
features.description: When one of the PCs splits from the group, the crowds shift and cut them off from the party.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 1 > Cliffside Ascent
entries.name: Cliffside Ascent

fields.description: A steep, rocky cliff side tall enough to make traversal dangerous.

features.name: The Climb
features.description: Climbing up the cliff side uses a Progress Countdown (12). It ticks down according to the following criteria when the PCs make an action roll to climb:

features.name: Pitons Left Behind
features.description: Previous climbers left behind large metal rods that climbers can use to aid their ascent. If a PC using the pitons fails an action roll to climb, they can mark a Stress instead of ticking the countdown up.

features.name: Fall
features.description: Spend a Fear to have a PC’s handhold fail,

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 1 > Local Tavern
entries.name: Local Tavern

fields.description: A lively tavern that serves as the social hub for its town.

features.name: What’s the Talk of the Town?
features.description: A PC can ask the bartender, staff , or patrons about local events, rumors, and potential work with a Presence Roll. On a success, they can pick two of the below details to learn—or three if they critically succeed. On a failure, they can pick one and mark a Stress as the local carries on about something irrelevant.

features.name: Sing For Your Supper
features.description: A PC can perform one time for the guests by making a Presence Roll. On a success, they earn 1d4 handfuls of gold (2d4 if they critically succeed). On a failure, they mark a Stress.

features.name: Mysterious Stranger
features.description: Reveal a stranger concealing their identity, lurking in a shaded booth.

features.name: Someone Comes to Town
features.description: Introduce a significant NPC who wants to hire the party for something or who relates to a PC’s background.

features.name: Bar Fight!
features.description: Spend a Fear to have a bar fight erupt in the tavern. When a PC tries to move through the tavern while the fight persists, they must succeed on an Agility or Presence Roll or take 1d6+2 physical damage from a wild swing or thrown object. A PC can try to activate this feature by succeeding on an action roll that would provoke tavern patrons.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 1 > Outpost Town
entries.name: Outpost Town

fields.description: A small town on the outskirts of a nation or region, close to a dungeon, tombs, or other adventuring destinations.

features.name: Rumors Abound
features.description: Gossip is the fastest-traveling currency in the realm. A PC can inquire about major events by making a Presence Roll. What they learn depends on the outcome of their roll, based on the following criteria:

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 1 > Raging River
entries.name: Raging River

fields.description: A swift-moving river without a bridge crossing, deep enough to sweep away most people.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 2 > Cult Ritual
entries.name: Cult Ritual

fields.description: A Fallen cult assembles around a sigil of the defeated gods and a bonfire that burns a sickly shade of green.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 2 > Hallowed Temple
entries.name: Hallowed Temple

fields.description: A bustling but well-kept temple that provides healing and hosts regular services, overseen by a priest or seraph.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 2 > Haunted City
entries.name: Haunted City

fields.description: An abandoned city populated by the restless spirits of eras past. Impulses: Misdirect and disorient, replay apocalypses both public and personal

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 1 > Acid Burrower
entries.name: Acid Burrower

fields.description: A horse-sized insect with digging claws and acidic blood.

features.name: Relentless (3)
features.description: The Burrower can be spotlighted up to three times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Earth Eruption
features.description: Mark a Stress to have the Burrower burst out of the ground. All creatures within Very Close range must succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll or be knocked over, making them Vulnerable until they next act.

features.name: Spit Acid
features.description: Make an attack against all targets in front of the Burrower within Close range. Targets the Burrower succeeds against take 2d6 physical damage and must mark an Armor Slot without receiving its benefits (they can still use armor to reduce the damage). If they can’t mark an Armor Slot, they must mark an additional HP and you gain a Fear.

features.name: Acid Bath
features.description: When the Burrower takes Severe damage, all creatures within Close range are bathed in their acidic blood, taking 1d10 physical damage. This splash covers the ground within Very Close range with blood, and all creatures other than the Burrower who move through it take 1d6 physical damage.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 1 > Bear
entries.name: Bear

fields.description: A large bear with thick fur and powerful claws.

features.name: Overwhelming Force
features.description: Targets who mark HP from the Bear’s standard attack are knocked back to Very Close range.

features.name: Bite
features.description: Mark a Stress to make an attack against a target within Melee range. On a success, deal 3d4+10 physical damage and the target is Restrained until they break free with a successful Strength Roll.

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When the Bear makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 1 > Cave Ogre
entries.name: Cave Ogre

fields.description: A massive humanoid who sees all sapient life as food.
fields.motives: Bite off heads, feast, rip limbs, stomp, throw enemies

features.name: Ramp Up
features.description: You must spend a Fear to spotlight the Ogre. While spotlighted, they can make their standard attack against all targets within range.

features.name: Bone Breaker
features.description: The Ogre’s attacks deal direct damage.

features.name: Hail of Boulders
features.description: Mark a Stress to pick up heavy objects and throw them at all targets in front of the Ogre within Far range. Make an attack against these targets. Targets the Ogre succeeds against take 1d10+2 physical damage. If they succeed against more than one target, you gain a Fear.

features.name: Rampaging Fury
features.description: When the Ogre marks 2 or more HP, they can rampage. Move the Ogre to a point within Close range and deal 2d6+3 direct physical damage to all targets in their path.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 1 > Construct
entries.name: Construct

fields.description: A roughly humanoid being of stone and steel, assembled and animated by magic.
fields.motives: Destroy environment, serve creator, smash target, trample groups

features.name: Relentless (2)
features.description: The Construct can be spotlighted up to two times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Weak Structure
features.description: When the Construct marks HP from physical damage, they must mark an additional HP.

features.name: Trample
features.description: Mark a Stress to make an attack against all targets in the Construct’s path when they move. Targets the Construct succeeds against take 1d8 physical damage.

features.name: Overload
features.description: Before rolling damage for the Construct’s attack, you can mark a Stress to gain a +10 bonus to the damage roll. The Construct can then take the spotlight again.

features.name: Death Quake
features.description: When the Construct marks their last HP, the magic powering them ruptures in an explosion of force. Make an attack with advantage against all targets within Very Close range. Targets the Construct succeeds against take 1d12+2 magic damage.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 1 > Courtier
entries.name: Courtier

fields.description: An ambitious and ostentatiously dressed socialite.

features.name: Mockery
features.description: Mark a Stress to say something mocking and force a target within Close range to make a Presence Reaction Roll (14) to see if they can save face. On a failure, the target must mark 2 Stress and is Vulnerable until the scene ends.

features.name: Scapegoat
features.description: Spend a Fear and target a PC. The Courtier convinces a crowd or prominent individual that the target is the cause of their current conflict or misfortune.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 1 > Deeproot Defender
entries.name: Deeproot Defender

fields.description: A burly vegetable-person with grasping vines.

features.name: Ground Slam
features.description: Slam the ground, knocking all targets within Very Close range back to Far range. Each target knocked back this way must mark a Stress.

features.name: Grab and Drag
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Close range. On a success, spend a Fear to pull them into Melee range, deal 1d6+2 physical damage, and Restrain them until the Defender takes Severe damage.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 1 > Dire Wolf
entries.name: Dire Wolf

fields.description: A large wolf with menacing teeth, seldom encountered alone.
fields.motives: Defend territory, harry, protect pack, surround, trail

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 2 > Mountain Pass
entries.name: Mountain Pass

fields.description: Stony peaks that pierce the clouds, with a twisting path winding its way up and over through many switchbacks.

features.name: Engraved Sigils
features.description: Large markings and engravings have been made in the mountainside. A PC with a relevant background or Experience identifies them as weather magic increasing the power of the icy winds. A PC who succeeds on a Knowledge Roll can recall information about the sigils, potential information about their creators, and the knowledge of how to dispel them. If a PC critically succeeds, they recognize that the sigils are of a style created by ridgeborne enchanters and they gain advantage on a roll to dispel the sigils.

features.name: Avalanche
features.description: Spend a Fear to carve the mountain with an icy torrent, causing an avalanche. All PCs in its path must succeed on an Agility or Strength Reaction Roll or be bowled over and carried down the mountain. A PC using rope, pitons, or other climbing gear gains advantage on this roll. Targets who fail are knocked down the mountain to Far range, take 2d20 physical damage, and must mark a Stress. Targets who succeed must mark a Stress.

features.name: Raptor Nest
features.description: When the PCs enter the raptors’ hunting grounds, two Giant Eagles appear at Very Far range of a chosen PC, identifying the PCs as likely prey.

features.name: Icy Winds
features.description: Countdown (Loop 4). When the PCs enter the mountain pass, activate the countdown. When it triggers, all characters traveling through the pass must succeed on a Strength Reaction Roll or mark a Stress. A PC wearing clothes appropriate for extreme cold gains advantage on these rolls.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 3 > Burning Heart Of The Woods
entries.name: Burning Heart Of The Woods

fields.description: Thick indigo ash fi lls the air around a towering moss-covered tree that burns eternally with flames a sickly shade of blue.

features.name: Chaos Magic Locus
features.description: When a PC makes a Spellcast Roll, they must roll two Fear Dice and take the higher result.

features.name: The Indigo Flame
features.description: PCs who approach the central tree can make a Knowledge Roll to try to identify the magic that consumed this environment.

features.name: Grasping Vines
features.description: Animate vines bristling with thorns whip out from the underbrush to ensnare the PCs. A target must succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll or become Restrained and Vulnerable until they break free, clearing both conditions, with a successful Finesse or Strength Roll or by dealing 10 damage to the vines. When the target makes a roll to escape, they take

features.name: Charcoal Constructs
features.description: Warped animals wreathed in indigo flame trample through a point of your choice. All targets within Close range of that point must make an Agility Reaction Roll. Targets who fail take 3d12+3 physical damage. Targets who succeed take half damage instead.

features.name: Choking Ash
features.description: Countdown (Loop ). When the PCs enter the Burning Heart of the Woods, activate the countdown. When it triggers, all characters must make a Strength or Instinct Reaction Roll. Targets who fail take 4d6+5 direct physical damage. Targets who succeed take half damage. Protective masks or clothes give advantage on the reaction roll.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 3 > Castle Siege
entries.name: Castle Siege

fields.description: An active siege with an attacking force fighting to gain entry to a fortified castle.

features.name: Secret Entrance
features.description: A PC can find or recall a secret way into the castle with a successful Instinct or Knowledge Roll.

features.name: Siege Weapons (Environment Change)
features.description: Consequence

features.name: Reinforcements!
features.description: Summon a Knight of the Realm, a number of Tier 3 Minions equal to the number of PCs, and two adversaries of your choice within Far range of a chosen PC as reinforcements. The Knight of the Realm immediately takes the spotlight.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 3 > Pitched Battle
entries.name: Pitched Battle

fields.description: A massive combat between two large groups of armed combatants.

features.name: Adrift on a Sea of Steel
