features.description: Traversing a battlefield during an active combat is extremely dangerous. A PC must succeed on an Agility Roll to move at all, and can only go up to Close range on a success. If an adversary is within Melee range of them, they must mark a Stress to make an Agility Roll to move.

features.name: Raze and Pillage
features.description: The attacking force raises the stakes by lighting a fire, stealing a valuable asset, kidnapping an important person, or killing the populace.

features.name: War Magic
features.description: Spend a Fear as a mage from one side uses large-scale destructive magic. Pick a point on the battlefield within Very Far range of the mage. All targets within Close range of that point must make an Agility Reaction Roll. Targets who fail take 3d12+8 magic damage and must mark a Stress.

features.name: Reinforcements!
features.description: Summon a Knight of the Realm, a number of Tier 3 Minions equal to the number of PCs, and two adversaries of your choice within Far range of a chosen PC as reinforcements. The Knight of the Realm immediately takes the spotlight.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 4 > Chaos Realm
entries.name: Chaos Realm

fields.description: An otherworldly space where the laws of reality are unstable and dangerous.

features.name: Impossible Architecture
features.description: Up is down, down is right, right is starward. Gravity and directionality themselves are in flux, and any attempt to move through this realm is an odyssey unto itself, requiring a Progress Countdown (8). On a failure, a PC must mark a Stress in addition to the roll’s other consequences.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 4 > Divine Usurpation
entries.name: Divine Usurpation

fields.description: A massive ritual designed to breach the gates of the Hallows Above and unseat the New Gods themselves.

features.name: Final Preparations
features.description: When the environment first takes the spotlight, designate one adversary as the Usurper seeking to overthrow the gods. Activate a Long-Term Countdown (8) as the Usurper assembles what they need to conduct the ritual. When it triggers, spotlight this environment to use the “Beginning of the End” feature. While this environment remains in play, you can hold up to 15 Fear.

features.name: Divine Blessing
features.description: When a PC critically succeeds, they can spend 2 Hope to refresh an ability normally limited by uses (such as once per rest, once per session).

features.name: Defilers Abound
features.description: Spend 2 Fear to summon 1d4+2 Fallen Shock Troops that appear within Close range of the Usurper to assist their divine siege. Immediately spotlight the Shock Troop to use a “Group Attack” action.

features.name: Godslayer
features.description: If the Divine Siege Countdown (see “Beginning of the End”) has triggered, you can spend 3 Fear to describe the Usurper slaying one of the gods of the Hallows Above, feasting upon their power and growing stronger. The Usurper clears 2 HP. Increase their Difficulty, damage, attack modifier, or give them a new feature from the slain god.

features.name: Beginning of the End
features.description: When the “Final Preparations” long-term countdown triggers, the Usurper begins hammering on the gates of the Hallows themselves. Activate a Divine Siege Countdown (10). Spotlight the Usurper to describe the Usurper’s assault and tick down this countdown by 1. If the Usurper takes Major or greater damage, tick up the countdown by 1. When it triggers, the Usurper shatters the barrier between the Mortal Realm and the Hallows Above to slay the gods and take their place. You gain a Fear for each unmarked HP the Usurper has. You can immediately use the “Godslayer” feature without spending Fear to make an additional GM move.

features.name: Ritual Nexus
features.description: On any failure with Fear against the Usurper, the PC must mark 1d4 Stress from the backlash of magical power.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 4 > Imperial Court
entries.name: Imperial Court

fields.description: The majestic domain of a powerful empire, lavishly appointed with stolen treasures.

features.name: All Roads Lead Here
features.description: While in the Imperial Court, a PC has disadvantage on Presence Rolls made to take actions that don’t fit the imperial way of life or support the empire’s dominance.

features.name: Rival Vassals
features.description: The PCs can find imperial subjects, vassals, and supplicants in the court, each vying for favor, seeking proximity to power, exchanging favors for loyalty, and elevating their status above others’. Some might be desperate to undermine their rivals, while others might even be open to discussions that verge on sedition.

features.name: The Gravity of Empire
features.description: Spend a Fear to present a PC with a golden opportunity or off er to satisfy a major goal in exchange for obeying or supporting the empire. The target must make a Presence Reaction Roll. On a failure, they must mark all their Stress or accept the off er. If they have already marked all their Stress, they must accept the off er or exile themselves from the empire. On a success, they must mark 1d4 Stress as they’re taxed by temptation.

features.name: Imperial Decree
features.description: Spend a Fear to tick down a long-term countdown related to the empire’s agenda by 1d4. If this triggers the countdown, a proclamation related to the agenda is announced at court as the plan is executed.

features.name: Eyes Everywhere
features.description: On a result with Fear, you can spend a Fear to have someone loyal to the empire overhear seditious talk within the court. A PC must succeed on an Instinct Reaction Roll to notice that the group has been overheard so they can try to intercept the witness before the PCs are exposed.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 4 > Necromancer’S Ossuary
entries.name: Necromancer’S Ossuary

fields.description: A dusty crypt with a library, twisting corridors, and abundant sarcophagi, spattered with the blood of ill-fated invaders.

features.name: No Place for the Living
features.description: A feature or action that clears HP requires spending a Hope to use. If it already costs Hope, a PC must spend an additional Hope.

features.name: Centuries of Knowledge
features.description: A PC can investigate the library and laboratory and make a Knowledge Roll to learn information related to arcana, local history, and the Necromancer’s plans.

features.name: Skeletal Burst
features.description: All targets within Close range of a point you choose in this environment must succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll or take 4d8+8 physical damage from skeletal shrapnel as part of the ossuary detonates around them.

features.name: Aura of Death
features.description: Once per scene, roll a d4. Each undead within Far range of the Necromancer can clear HP and Stress equal to the result rolled. The undead can choose how that number is divided between HP and Stress.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 1 > Giant Mosquitoes
entries.name: Giant Mosquitoes

fields.description: Dozens of fist-sized mosquitoes, flying together for protection.

features.name: Horde (1d4+1)
features.description: When the Mosquitoes have marked half or more of their HP, their standard attack deals 1d4+1 physical damage instead.

features.name: Flying
features.description: While flying, the Mosquitoes have a +2 bonus to their Difficulty.

features.name: Bloodsucker
features.description: When the Mosquitoes’ attack causes a target to mark HP, you can mark a Stress to force the target to mark an additional HP.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 1 > Giant Rat
entries.name: Giant Rat

fields.description: A cat-sized rodent skilled at scavenging and survival.

features.name: Minion (3)
features.description: The Rat is defeated when they take any damage. For every 3 damage a PC deals to the Rat, defeat an additional Minion within range the attack would succeed against.

features.name: Group Attack
features.description: Spend a Fear to choose a target and spotlight all Giant Rats within Close range of them. Those Minions move into Melee range of the target and make one shared attack roll. On a success, they deal 1 physical damage each. Combine this damage.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 1 > Giant Scorpion
entries.name: Giant Scorpion

fields.description: A human-sized arachnid with tearing claws and a stinging tail.

features.name: Double Strike
features.description: Mark a Stress to make a standard attack against two targets within Melee range.

features.name: Venomous Stinger
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Very Close range. On a success, spend a Fear to deal 1d4+4 physical damage and Poison them until their next rest or they succeed on a Knowledge Roll (16). While Poisoned, the target must roll a d6 before they make an action roll. On a result of 4 or lower, they must mark a Stress.

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When the Scorpion makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 1 > Glass Snake
entries.name: Glass Snake

fields.description: A clear serpent with a massive head that leaves behind a glass shard trail wherever they go.

features.name: Armor-Shredding Shards
features.description: After a successful attack against the Snake within Melee range, the attacker must mark an Armor Slot. If they can’t mark an Armor Slot, they must mark an HP.

features.name: Spinning Serpent
features.description: Mark a Stress to make an attack against all targets within Very Close range. Targets the Snake succeeds against take 1d6+1 physical damage.

features.name: Spitter
features.description: Spend a Fear to introduce a d6 Spitter Die. When the Snake is in the spotlight, roll this die. On a result of 5 or higher, all targets in front of the Snake within Far range must succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll or take 1d4 physical damage. The Snake can take the spotlight a second time this GM turn.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 1 > Harrier
entries.name: Harrier

fields.description: A nimble fighter armed with javelins.

features.name: Maintain Distance
features.description: After making a standard attack, the Harrier can move anywhere within Far range.

features.name: Fall Back
features.description: When a creature moves into Melee range to make an attack, you can mark a Stress before the attack roll to move anywhere within Close range and make an attack against that creature. On a success, deal 1d10+2 physical damage.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 1 > Archer Guard
entries.name: Archer Guard

fields.description: A tall guard bearing a longbow and quiver with arrows fletched in the settlement’s colors.
fields.motives: Arrest, close gates, make it through the day, pin down

features.name: Hobbling Shot
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Far range. On a success, mark a Stress to deal 1d12+3 physical damage. If the target marks HP from this attack, they have disadvantage on Agility Rolls until they clear at least 1 HP.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 1 > Bladed Guard
entries.name: Bladed Guard

fields.description: An armored guard bearing a sword and shield painted in the settlement’s colors.
fields.motives: Arrest, close gates, make it through the day, pin down

features.name: Shield Wall
features.description: A creature who tries to move within Very Close range of the Guard must succeed on an Agility Roll. If additional Bladed Guards are standing in a line alongside the first, and each is within Melee range of another guard in the line, the Difficulty increases by the total number of guards in that line.

features.name: Detain
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Very Close range. On a success, mark a Stress to Restrain the target until they break free with a successful attack, Finesse Roll, or Strength Roll.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 1 > Head Guard
entries.name: Head Guard

fields.description: A seasoned guard with a mace, a whistle, and a bellowing voice.
fields.motives: Arrest, close gates, pin down, seek glory

features.name: Rally Guards
features.description: Spend 2 Fear to spotlight the Head Guard and up to 2d4 allies within Far range.

features.name: On My Signal
features.description: Countdown (5). When the Head Guard is in the spotlight for the first time, activate the countdown. It ticks down when a PC makes an attack roll. When it triggers, all Archer Guards within Far range make a standard attack with advantage against the nearest target within their range. If any attacks succeed on the same target, combine their damage.

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When the Head Guard makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 1 > Jagged Knife Bandit
entries.name: Jagged Knife Bandit

fields.description: A cunning criminal in a cloak bearing one of the gang’s iconic knives.

features.name: Climber
features.description: The Bandit climbs just as easily as they run.

features.name: From Above
features.description: When the Bandit succeeds on a standard attack from above a target, they deal 1d10+1 physical damage instead of their standard damage.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 1 > Jagged Knife Hexer
entries.name: Jagged Knife Hexer

fields.description: A staff -wielding bandit in a cloak adorned with magical paraphernalia, using curses to vex their foes.

features.name: Curse
features.description: Choose a target within Far range and temporarily Curse them. While the target is Cursed, you can mark a Stress when that target rolls with Hope to make the roll be with Fear instead.

features.name: Chaotic Flux
features.description: Make an attack against up to three targets within Very Close range. Mark a Stress to deal 2d6+3 magic damage to targets the Hexer succeeded against.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 1 > Jagged Knife Kneebreaker
entries.name: Jagged Knife Kneebreaker

fields.description: An imposing brawler carrying a large club.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 1 > Jagged Knife Lackey
entries.name: Jagged Knife Lackey

fields.description: A thief with simple clothes and small daggers, eager to prove themselves.

features.name: Minion (3)
features.description: The Lackey is defeated when they take any damage. For every 3 damage a PC deals to the Lackey, defeat an additional Minion within range the attack would succeed against.

features.name: Group Attack
features.description: Spend a Fear to choose a target and spotlight all Jagged Knife Lackeys within Close range of them. Those Minions move into Melee range of the target and make one shared attack roll. On a success, they deal 2 physical damage each. Combine this damage.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 1 > Jagged Knife Lieutenant
entries.name: Jagged Knife Lieutenant

fields.description: A seasoned bandit in quality leathers with a strong voice and cunning eyes.

features.name: Tactician
features.description: When you spotlight the Lieutenant, mark a

features.name: More Where That Came From
features.description: Summon three Jagged Knife Lackeys, who appear at Far range.

features.name: Coup de Grace
features.description: Spend a Fear to make an attack against a Vulnerable target within Close range. On a success, deal 2d6+12 physical damage and the target must mark a Stress.

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When the Lieutenant makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 1 > Jagged Knife Shadow
entries.name: Jagged Knife Shadow

fields.description: A nimble scoundrel bearing a wicked knife and utilizing shadow magic to isolate targets.

features.name: Backstab
features.description: When the Shadow succeeds on a standard attack that has advantage, they deal 1d6+6 physical damage instead of their standard damage.

features.name: Cloaked
features.description: Become Hidden until after the Shadow’s next attack. Attacks made while Hidden from this feature have advantage.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 1 > Jagged Knife Sniper
entries.name: Jagged Knife Sniper

fields.description: A lanky bandit striking from cover with a shortbow.

features.name: Unseen Strike
features.description: If the Sniper is Hidden when they make a successful standard attack against a target, they deal 1d10+4 physical damage instead of their standard damage.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 1 > Merchant
entries.name: Merchant

fields.description: A finely dressed trader with a keen eye for financial gain.
fields.motives: Buy low and sell high, create demand, inflate prices, seek profit

features.name: Preferential Treatment
features.description: A PC who succeeds on a Presence Roll against the Merchant gains a discount on purchases. A PC who fails on a Presence Roll against the Merchant must pay more and has disadvantage on future Presence Rolls against the Merchant.

features.name: The Runaround
features.description: When a PC rolls a 14 or lower on a Presence Roll made against the Merchant, they must mark a Stress.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 1 > Minor Chaos Elemental
entries.name: Minor Chaos Elemental

fields.description: A coruscating mass of uncontrollable magic.

features.name: Arcane Form
features.description: The Elemental is resistant to magic damage.

features.name: Sickening Flux
features.description: Mark a HP to force all targets within Close range to mark a Stress and become Vulnerable until their next rest or they clear a HP.

features.name: Remake Reality
features.description: Spend a Fear to transform the area within Very Close range into a diff erent biome. All targets within this area take 2d6+3 direct magic damage.

features.name: Magical Reflection
features.description: When the Elemental takes damage from an attack within Close range, deal an amount of damage to the attacker equal to half the damage they dealt.

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When the Elemental makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 1 > Minor Fire Elemental
entries.name: Minor Fire Elemental

fields.description: A living flame the size of a large bonfire.
fields.motives: Encircle enemies, grow in size, intimidate, start fires

features.name: Relentless (2)
features.description: The Elemental can be spotlighted up to two times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Scorched Earth
features.description: Mark a Stress to choose a point within Far range. The ground within Very Close range of that point immediately bursts into flames. All creatures within this area must make an Agility Reaction Roll. Targets who fail take 2d8 magic damage from the flames. Targets who succeed take half damage.

features.name: Explosion
features.description: Spend a Fear to erupt in a fiery explosion. Make an attack against all targets within Close range. Targets the Elemental succeeds against take 1d8 magic damage and are knocked back to Far range.

features.name: Consume Kindling
features.description: Three times per scene, when the Elemental moves onto objects that are highly flammable, consume them to clear a HP or a Stress.

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When the Elemental makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 1 > Minor Demon
entries.name: Minor Demon

fields.description: A crimson-hued creature from the Circles Below, consumed by rage against all mortals.
fields.motives: Act erratically, corral targets, relish pain, torment

features.name: Relentless (2)
features.description: The Demon can be spotlighted up to two times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: All Must Fall
features.description: When a PC rolls a failure with Fear while within Close range of the Demon, they lose a Hope.

features.name: Hellfire
features.description: Spend a Fear to rain down hellfire within Far range. All targets within the area must make an Agility Reaction Roll. Targets who fail take 1d20+3 magic damage. Targets who succeed take half damage.

features.name: Reaper
features.description: Before rolling damage for the Demon’s attack, you can mark a Stress to gain a bonus to the damage roll equal to the Demon’s current number of marked HP.

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When the Demon makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 1 > Minor Treant
entries.name: Minor Treant

fields.description: An ambulatory sapling rising up to defend their forest.

features.name: Minion (5)
features.description: The Treant is defeated when they take any damage. For every 5 damage a PC deals to the Treant, defeat an additional Minion within range the attack would succeed against.

features.name: Group Attack
features.description: Spend a Fear to choose a target and spotlight all Minor Treants within Close range of them. Those Minions move into Melee range of the target and make one shared attack roll. On a success, they deal 4 physical damage each. Combine this damage.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 1 > Green Ooze
entries.name: Green Ooze

fields.description: A moving mound of translucent green slime.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 1 > Tiny Green Ooze
entries.name: Tiny Green Ooze

fields.description: A small moving mound of translucent green slime.

features.name: Acidic Form
features.description: When the Ooze makes a successful attack, the target must mark an Armor Slot without receiving its benefits (they can still use armor to reduce the damage). If they can’t mark an Armor Slot, they must mark an additional HP.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 1 > Red Ooze
entries.name: Red Ooze

fields.description: A moving mound of translucent flaming red slime.
fields.motives: Camouflage, consume and multiply, ignite, start fires

features.name: Creeping Fire
features.description: The Ooze can only move within Very Close range as their normal movement. They light any flammable object they touch on fire.

features.name: Ignite
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Very Close range. On a success, the target takes 1d8 magic damage and is Ignited until they’re extinguished with a successful Finesse Roll (14). While Ignited, the target takes 1d4 magic damage when they make an action roll.

features.name: Split
features.description: When the Ooze has 3 or more HP marked, you can spend a Fear to split them into two Tiny Red Oozes (with no marked HP or Stress). Immediately spotlight both of them.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 1 > Tiny Red Ooze
entries.name: Tiny Red Ooze

fields.description: A small moving mound of translucent flaming red slime

features.name: Burning
features.description: When a creature within Melee range deals damage to the Ooze, they take 1d6 direct magic damage.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 1 > Petty Noble
entries.name: Petty Noble

fields.description: A richly dressed and adorned aristocrat brimming with hubris.
fields.motives: Abuse power, gather resources, mobilize minions

features.name: My Land, My Rules
features.description: All social actions made against the Noble on their land have disadvantage.

features.name: Guards, Seize Them!
features.description: Once per scene, mark a Stress to summon 1d4 Bladed Guards, who appear at Far range to enforce the Noble’s will.

features.name: Exile
features.description: Spend a Fear and target a PC. The Noble proclaims that the target and their allies are exiled from the noble’s territory. While exiled, the target and their allies have disadvantage during social situations within the Noble’s domain.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 1 > Pirate Captain
entries.name: Pirate Captain

fields.description: A charismatic sea dog with an impressive hat, eager to raid and plunder.
fields.motives: Command, make ’em walk the plank, plunder, raid

features.name: Swashbuckler
features.description: When the Captain marks 2 or fewer HP from an attack within Melee range, the attacker must mark a Stress.

features.name: Reinforcements
features.description: Once per scene, mark a Stress to summon a Pirate Raiders Horde, which appears at Far range.

features.name: No Quarter
features.description: Spend a Fear to choose a target who has three or more Pirates within Melee range of them. The Captain leads the Pirates in hurling threats and promises of a watery grave. The target must make a Presence Reaction Roll. On a failure, the target marks 1d4+1 Stress. On a success, they must mark a Stress.

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When the Captain makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 1 > Pirate Raiders
entries.name: Pirate Raiders

fields.description: Seafaring scoundrels moving in a ravaging pack.

features.name: Horde (1d4+1)
features.description: When the Raiders have marked half or more of their HP, their standard attack deals 1d4+1 physical damage instead.

features.name: Swashbuckler
features.description: When the Raiders mark 2 or fewer HP from an attack within Melee range, the attacker must mark a Stress.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 1 > Pirate Tough
entries.name: Pirate Tough

fields.description: A thickly muscled and tattooed pirate with melon-sized fists.

features.name: Swashbuckler
features.description: When the Tough marks 2 or fewer HP from an attack within Melee range, the attacker must mark a Stress.

features.name: Clear the Decks
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Very Close range. On a success, mark a Stress to move into Melee range of the target, dealing 3d4 physical damage and knocking the target back to Close range.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 1 > Sellsword
entries.name: Sellsword

fields.description: An armed mercenary testing their luck.

features.name: Minion (4)
features.description: The Sellsword is defeated when they take any damage. For every 4 damage a PC deals to the Sellsword, defeat an additional Minion within range the attack would succeed against.

features.name: Group Attack
features.description: Spend a Fear to choose a target and spotlight all Sellswords within Close range of them. Those Minions move into Melee range of the target and make one shared attack roll. On a success, they deal 3 physical damage each. Combine this damage.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 1 > Skeleton Archer
entries.name: Skeleton Archer

fields.description: A fragile skeleton with a shortbow and arrows.
fields.motives: Perforate distracted targets, play dead, steal skin

features.name: Opportunist
features.description: When two or more adversaries are within Very Close range of a creature, all damage the Archer deals to that creature is doubled.

features.name: Deadly Shot
features.description: Make an attack against a Vulnerable target within Far range. On a success, mark a Stress to deal 3d4+8 physical damage.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 1 > Skeleton Dredge
entries.name: Skeleton Dredge

fields.description: A clattering pile of bones.
fields.motives: Fall apart, overwhelm, play dead, steal skin

features.name: Minion (4)
features.description: The Dredge is defeated when they take any damage. For every 4 damage a PC deals to the Dredge, defeat an additional Minion within range the attack would succeed against.

features.name: Group Attack
features.description: Spend a Fear to choose a target and spotlight all Dredges within Close range of them. Those Minions move into Melee range of the target and make one shared attack roll. On a success, they deal 1 physical damage each. Combine this damage.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 1 > Skeleton Warrior
entries.name: Skeleton Warrior

fields.description: A dirt-covered skeleton armed with a rusted blade.
fields.motives: Feign death, gang up, steal skin

features.name: Only Bones
features.description: The Warrior is resistant to physical damage.

features.name: Won’t Stay Dead
features.description: When the Warrior is defeated, you can spotlight them and roll a d6. On a result of 6, if there are other adversaries on the battlefield, the Warrior re-forms with no marked HP.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 1 > Spellblade
entries.name: Spellblade

fields.description: A mercenary combining swordplay and magic to deadly eff ect.

features.name: Arcane Steel
features.description: Damage dealt by the Spellblade’s standard attack is considered both physical and magic.

features.name: Suppressing Blast
features.description: Mark a Stress and target a group within Far range. All targets must succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll or take 1d8+2 magic damage. You gain a Fear for each target who marked HP from this attack.

features.name: Move as a Unit
features.description: Spend 2 Fear to spotlight up to five allies within Far range.

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When the Spellblade makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 1 > Swarm Of Rats
entries.name: Swarm Of Rats

fields.description: A skittering mass of ordinary rodents moving as one like a ravenous wave.

features.name: Horde (1d4+1)
features.description: When the Swarm has marked half or more of their HP, their standard attack deals 1d4+1 physical damage instead.

features.name: In Your Face
features.description: All targets within Melee range have disadvantage on attacks against targets other than the Swarm.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 1 > Sylvan Soldier
entries.name: Sylvan Soldier

fields.description: A faerie warrior adorned in armor made of leaves and bark.

features.name: Pack Tactics
features.description: If the Soldier makes a standard attack and another Sylvan Soldier is within Melee range of the target, deal 1d8+5 physical damage instead of their standard damage.

features.name: Forest Control
features.description: Spend a Fear to pull down a tree within Close range. A creature hit by the tree must succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll (15) or take 1d10 physical damage.

features.name: Blend In
features.description: When the Soldier makes a successful attack, you can mark a Stress to become Hidden until the Soldier’s next attack or a PC succeeds on an Instinct Roll (14) to find them.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 1 > Tangle Bramble Swarm
entries.name: Tangle Bramble Swarm

fields.description: A cluster of animate, blood-drinking tumbleweeds, each the size of a large gourd.

features.name: Horde (1d4+2)
features.description: When the Swarm has marked half or more of their HP, their standard attack deals 1d4+2 physical damage instead.

features.name: Crush
features.description: Mark a Stress to deal 2d6+8 direct physical damage to a target with 3 or more bramble tokens.

features.name: Encumber
features.description: When the Swarm succeeds on an attack, give the target a bramble token. If a target has any bramble tokens, they are Restrained. If a target has 3 or more bramble tokens, they are also Vulnerable. All bramble tokens can be removed by succeeding on a Finesse Roll ( 1 2 + the number of bramble tokens) or dealing Major or greater damage to the Swarm. If bramble tokens are removed from a target using a Finesse Roll, a number of Tangle Bramble Minions spawn within Melee range equal to the number of tokens removed.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 1 > Tangle Bramble
entries.name: Tangle Bramble

fields.description: An animate, blood-drinking tumbleweed.

features.name: Minion (4)
features.description: The Bramble is defeated when they take any damage. For every 4 damage a PC deals to the Tangle Bramble, defeat an additional Minion within range the attack would succeed against.

features.name: Group Attack
features.description: Spend a Fear to choose a target and spotlight all Tangle Brambles within Close range of them. Those Minions move into Melee range of the target and make one shared attack roll. On a success, they deal 2 physical damage each. Combine this damage.

features.name: Drain and Multiply
features.description: When an attack from the Bramble causes a target to mark HP and there are three or more Tangle Bramble Minions within Close range, you can combine the Minions into a Tangle Bramble Swarm Horde. The Horde’s HP is equal to the number of Minions combined.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 1 > Weaponmaster
entries.name: Weaponmaster

fields.description: A master-at-arms wielding a sword twice their size.
fields.motives: Act first, aim for the weakest, intimidate

features.name: Goading Strike
features.description: Make a standard attack against a target. On a success, mark a Stress to Taunt the target until their next successful attack. The next time the Taunted target attacks, they have disadvantage against targets other than the Weaponmaster.

features.name: Adrenaline Burst
features.description: Once per scene, spend a Fear to clear 2 HP and 2 Stress.

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When the Weaponmaster makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 1 > Young Dryad
entries.name: Young Dryad

fields.description: An imperious tree-person leading their forest’s defenses.

features.name: Voice of the Forest
features.description: Mark a Stress to spotlight 1d4 allies within range of a target they can attack without moving. On a success, their attacks deal half damage.

features.name: Thorny Cage
features.description: Spend a Fear to form a cage around a target within Very Close range and Restrain them until they’re freed with a successful Strength Roll. When a creature makes an action roll against the cage, they must mark a Stress.

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When the Dryad makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 1 > Brawny Zombie
entries.name: Brawny Zombie

fields.description: A large corpse, decay-bloated and angry.

features.name: Slow
features.description: When you spotlight the Zombie and they don’t have a token on their stat block, they can’t act yet. Place a token on their stat block and describe what they’re preparing to do. When you spotlight the Zombie and they have a token on their stat block, clear the token and they can act.

features.name: Rend Asunder
features.description: Make a standard attack with advantage against a target the Zombie has Restrained. On a success, the attack deals direct damage.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 1 > Patchwork Zombie Hulk
entries.name: Patchwork Zombie Hulk

fields.description: A towering gestalt of corpses moving as one, with torso-sized limbs and fists as large as a grown halfling.
fields.attack: +4 | Too Many Arms: Very Close | 1d20 phy

features.name: Destructible
features.description: When the Zombie takes Major or greater damage, they mark an additional HP.

features.name: Flailing Limbs
features.description: When the Zombie makes a standard attack, they can attack all targets within Very Close range.

features.name: Another for the Pile
features.description: When the Zombie is within Very Close range of a corpse, they can incorporate it into themselves, clearing a HP and a Stress.

features.name: Tormented Screams
features.description: Mark a Stress to cause all PCs within Far range to make a Presence Reaction Roll (13). Targets who fail lose a Hope and you gain a Fear for each. Targets who succeed must mark a Stress.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 1 > Rotted Zombie
entries.name: Rotted Zombie

fields.description: A decaying corpse ambling toward their prey.

features.name: Minion (3)
features.description: The Zombie is defeated when they take any damage. For every 3 damage a PC deals to the Zombie defeat an additional Minion within range the attack would succeed against.

features.name: Group Attack
features.description: Spend a Fear to choose a target and spotlight all Rotted Zombies within Close range of them. Those Minions move into Melee range of the target and make one shared attack roll. On a success, they deal 2 physical damage each. Combine this damage.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 1 > Shambling Zombie
entries.name: Shambling Zombie

fields.description: An animated corpse that moves shakily, driven only by hunger.
fields.motives: Devour, hungry, mob enemy, shred flesh

features.name: Too Many to Handle
features.description: When the Zombie is within Melee range of a creature and at least one other Zombie is within Close range, all attacks against that creature have advantage.

features.name: Horrifying
features.description: Targets who mark HP from the Zombie’s attacks must also mark a Stress.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 1 > Zombie Pack
entries.name: Zombie Pack

fields.description: A group of shambling corpses instinctively moving together.

features.name: Horde (1d4+2)
features.description: When the Zombies have marked half or more of their HP, their standard attack deals 1d4+2 physical damage instead.

features.name: Overwhelm
features.description: When the Zombies mark HP from an attack within Melee range, you can mark a Stress to make a standard attack against the attacker.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 2 > Archer Squadron
entries.name: Archer Squadron

fields.description: A group of trained archers bearing massive bows.

features.name: Horde (1d6+3)
features.description: When the Squadron has marked half or more of their HP, their standard attack deals 1d6+3 physical damage instead.

features.name: Focused Volley
features.description: Spend a Fear to target a point within Far range. Make an attack with advantage against all targets within Close range of that point. Targets the Squadron succeeds against take 1d10+4 physical damage.

features.name: Suppressing Fire
features.description: Mark a Stress to target a point within Far range. Until the next roll with Fear, a creature who moves within Close range of that point must make an Agility Reaction Roll. On a failure, they take 2d6+3 physical damage. On a success, they take half damage.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 2 > Apprentice Assassin
entries.name: Apprentice Assassin

fields.description: A young trainee eager to prove themselves.
fields.motives: Act reckless, kill, prove their worth, show off

features.name: Minion (6)
features.description: The Assassin is defeated when they take any damage. For every 6 damage a PC deals to the Assassin, defeat an additional Minion within range the attack would succeed against.

features.name: Group Attack
features.description: Spend a Fear to choose a target and spotlight all Apprentice Assassins within Close range of them. Those Minions move into Melee range of the target and make one shared attack roll. On a success, they deal 4 physical damage each. Combine this damage.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 2 > Assassin Poisoner
entries.name: Assassin Poisoner

fields.description: A cunning scoundrel skilled in both poisons and ambushing.
fields.motives: Anticipate, get paid, kill, taint food and water

features.name: Grindletooth Venom
features.description: Targets who mark HP from the

features.name: Out of Nowhere
features.description: The Assassin has advantage on attacks if they are Hidden.

features.name: Fumigation
features.description: Drop a smoke bomb that fills the air within Close range with smoke, Dizzying all targets in this area. Dizzied targets have disadvantage on their next action roll, then clear the condition.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 2 > Master Assassin
entries.name: Master Assassin

fields.description: A seasoned killer with a threatening voice and a deadly blade.
fields.motives: Ambush, get out alive, kill, prepare for all scenarios

features.name: Won’t See It Coming
features.description: The Assassin deals direct damage while they’re Hidden.

features.name: Strike as One
features.description: Mark a Stress to spotlight a number of other Assassins equal to the Assassin’s unmarked Stress.

features.name: The Subtle Blade
features.description: When the Assassin successfully makes a standard attack against a Vulnerable target, you can spend a Fear to deal Severe damage instead of their standard damage.

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When the Assassin makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 2 > Battle Box
entries.name: Battle Box

fields.description: A cube-shaped construct with a diff erent rune on each of their six sides.
fields.motives: Change tactics, trample foes, wait in disguise

features.name: Relentless (2)
features.description: The Box can be spotlighted up to two times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Randomized Tactics
features.description: Mark a Stress and roll a d6. The Box uses the corresponding move:

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 2 > Chaos Skull
entries.name: Chaos Skull

fields.description: A floating humanoid skull animated by scintillating magic.

features.name: Levitation
features.description: The Skull levitates several feet off the ground and can’t be Restrained.

features.name: Wards
features.description: The Skull is resistant to magic damage.

features.name: Magic Burst
features.description: Mark a Stress to make an attack against all targets within Close range. Targets the Skull succeeds against take 2d6+4 magic damage.

features.name: Siphon Magic
features.description: Spend a Fear to make an attack against a PC with a Spellcast trait within Very Close range. On a success, the target marks 1d4 Stress and the Skull clears that many Stress. Additionally, on a success, the Skull can immediately be spotlighted again.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 2 > Conscript
entries.name: Conscript

fields.description: A poorly trained civilian pressed into war.

features.name: Minion (6)
features.description: The Conscript is defeated when they take any damage. For every 6 damage a PC deals to the Conscript, defeat an additional Minion within range the attack would succeed against.

features.name: Group Attack
features.description: Spend a Fear to choose a target and spotlight all Conscripts within Close range of them. Those Minions move into Melee range of the target and make one shared attack roll. On a success, they deal 6 physical damage each. Combine this damage.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 2 > Courtesan
entries.name: Courtesan

fields.description: An accomplished manipulator and master of the social arts.

features.name: Searing Glance
features.description: When a PC within Close range makes a Presence Roll, you can mark a Stress to cast a gaze toward the aftermath. On the target’s failure, they must mark 2 Stress and are Vulnerable until the scene ends or they succeed on a social action against the Courtesan. On the target’s success, they must mark a Stress.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 2 > Cult Adept
entries.name: Cult Adept

fields.description: An experienced mage wielding shadow and fear.
fields.motives: Curry favor, hinder foes, uncover knowledge

features.name: Enervating Blast
features.description: Spend a Fear to make a standard attack against a target within range. On a success, the target must mark a Stress.

features.name: Shroud of the Fallen
features.description: Mark a Stress to wrap an ally within Close range in a shroud of Protection until the Adept marks their last HP. While Protected, the target has resistance to all damage.

features.name: Shadow Shackles
features.description: Spend a Fear and choose a point within Far range. All targets within Close range of that point are Restrained in smoky chains until they break free with a successful Strength or Instinct Roll. A target Restrained by this feature must spend a Hope to make an action roll.

features.name: Fear Is Fuel
features.description: Twice per scene, when a PC rolls a failure with Fear, clear a Stress.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 2 > Cult Fang
entries.name: Cult Fang

fields.description: A professional killer-turned-cultist.
fields.motives: Capture sacrifices, isolate prey, rise in the ranks

features.name: Shadow’s Embrace
features.description: The Fang can climb and walk on vertical surfaces. Mark a Stress to move from one shadow to another within Far range.

features.name: Pick Off the Straggler
features.description: Mark a Stress to cause a target within Melee range to make an Instinct Reaction Roll. On a failure, the target must mark 2 Stress and is teleported with the Fang to a shadow within Far range, making them temporarily Vulnerable. On a success, the target must mark a Stress.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 2 > Cult Initiate
entries.name: Cult Initiate

fields.description: A low-ranking cultist in simple robes, eager to gain power.
fields.motives: Follow orders, gain power, seek forbidden knowledge

features.name: Minion (6)
features.description: The Initiate is defeated when they take any damage. For every 6 damage a PC deals to the Initiate, defeat an additional Minion within range the attack would succeed against.

features.name: Group Attack
features.description: Spend a Fear to choose a target and spotlight all Cult Initiates within Close range of them. Those Minions move into Melee range of the target and make one shared attack roll. On a success, they deal 5 physical damage each. Combine this damage.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 2 > Demonic Hound Pack
entries.name: Demonic Hound Pack

fields.description: Unnatural hounds lit from within by hellfire.
fields.motives: Cause fear, consume flesh, please masters

features.name: Horde (2d4+1)
features.description: When the Pack has marked half or more of their HP, their standard attack deals 2d4+1 physical damage instead.

features.name: Dreadhowl
features.description: Mark a Stress to make all targets within Very Close range lose a Hope. If a target is not able to lose a Hope, they must instead mark 2 Stress.

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When the Pack makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 2 > Electric Eels
entries.name: Electric Eels

fields.description: A swarm of eels that encircle and electrocute.
fields.motives: Avoid larger predators, shock prey, tear apart

features.name: Horde (2d4+1)
features.description: When the Eels have marked half or more of their HP, their standard attack deals 2d4+1 physical damage instead.

features.name: Paralyzing Shock
features.description: Mark a Stress to make a standard attack against all targets within Very Close range. You gain a Fear for each target that marks HP.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 2 > Elite Soldier
entries.name: Elite Soldier

fields.description: An armored squire or experienced commoner looking to advance.
fields.motives: Gain glory, keep order, make alliances

features.name: Reinforce
features.description: Mark a Stress to move into Melee range of an ally and make a standard attack against a target within Very Close range. On a success, deal d10+2 physical damage and the ally can clear a Stress.

features.name: Vassal’s Loyalty
features.description: When the Soldier is within Very Close range of a knight or other noble who would take damage, you can mark a Stress to move into Melee range of them and take the damage instead.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 2 > Failed Experiment
entries.name: Failed Experiment

fields.description: A magical necromantic experiment gone wrong, leaving them warped and ungainly.

features.name: Warped Fortitude
features.description: The Experiment is resistant to physical damage.

features.name: Overwhelm
features.description: When a target the Experiment attacks has other adversaries within Very Close range, the Experiment deals double damage.

features.name: Lurching Lunge
features.description: Mark a Stress to spotlight the Experiment as an additional GM move instead of spending Fear.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 2 > Giant Beastmaster
entries.name: Giant Beastmaster

fields.description: A leather-clad warrior bearing a whip and massive bow.
fields.motives: Command, make a living, maneuver, pin down, protect companion animals

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 2 > Giant Brawler
entries.name: Giant Brawler

fields.description: An especially muscular giant wielding a warhammer larger than a human.
fields.motives: Make a living, overwhelm, slam, topple

features.name: Battering Ram
features.description: Mark a Stress to have the Brawler charge at an inanimate object within Close range they could feasibly smash (such as a wall, cart, or market stand) and destroy it. All targets within Very Close range of the object must succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll or take 2d4+3 physical damage from the shrapnel.

features.name: Bloody Reprisal
features.description: When the Brawler marks 2 or more HP from an attack within Very Close range, you can make a standard attack against the attacker. On a success, the Brawler deals 2d6+15 physical damage instead of their standard damage.

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When the Brawler makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 2 > Giant Recruit
entries.name: Giant Recruit

fields.description: A giant fi ghter wearing borrowed armor.
fields.motives: Batter, make a living, overwhelm, terrify

features.name: Minion (7)
features.description: The Recruit is defeated when they take any damage. For every 7 damage a PC deals to the Recruit, defeat an additional Minion within range the attack would succeed against.

features.name: Group Attack
features.description: Spend a Fear to choose a target and spotlight all Giant Recruits within Close range of them. Those Minions move into Melee range of the target and make one shared attack roll. On a success, they deal 5 physical damage each. Combine this damage.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 2 > Giant Eagle
entries.name: Giant Eagle

fields.description: A giant bird of prey with blood-stained talons.
fields.motives: Hunt prey, stay mobile, strike decisively
fields.attack: +1 | Claws and Beak: Very Close | 2d6+3 phy

features.name: Flight
features.description: While flying, the Eagle gains a + 3 bonus to their Difficulty.

features.name: Deadly Dive
features.description: Mark a Stress to attack a target within Far range. On a success, deal 2d10+2 physical damage and knock the target over, making them Vulnerable until they next act.

features.name: Take Off
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Very Close range. On a success, deal 2d4+3 physical damage and the target must succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll or become temporarily Restrained within the Eagle’s massive talons. If the target is Restrained, the Eagle immediately lifts into the air to Very Far range above the battlefield while holding them.

features.name: Deadly Drop
features.description: While flying, the Eagle can drop a Restrained target they are holding. When dropped, the target is no longer Restrained but starts falling. If their fall isn’t prevented during the PCs’ next action, the target takes 2d20 physical damage when they land.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 2 > Gorgon
entries.name: Gorgon

fields.description: A snake-headed, scaled humanoid with a gilded bow, enraged that their peace has been disturbed.

features.name: Relentless (2)
features.description: The Gorgon can be spotlighted up to two times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Sunsear Arrows
features.description: When the Gorgon makes a successful standard attack, the target Glows until the end of the scene and can’t become Hidden. Attack rolls made against a Glowing target have advantage.

features.name: Crown of Serpents
features.description: Make an attack roll against a target within Melee range using the Gorgon’s protective snakes. On a success, mark a Stress to deal 2d10+4 physical damage and the target must mark a Stress.

features.name: Petrifying Gaze
features.description: When the Gorgon takes damage from an attack within Close range, you can spend a Fear to force the attacker to make an Instinct Reaction Roll. On a failure, they begin to turn to stone, marking a HP and starting a Petrification Countdown (4). This countdown ticks down when the Gorgon is attacked. When it triggers, the target must make a death move. If the Gorgon is defeated, all petrification countdowns end.

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When the Gorgon makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 2 > Juvenile Flickerfly
entries.name: Juvenile Flickerfly

fields.description: A horse-sized insect with iridescent scales and crystalline wings moving faster than the eye can see.

features.name: Relentless (3)
features.description: The Flickerfly can be spotlighted up to three times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Peerless Accuracy
features.description: Before the Flickerfly makes an attack, roll a d6. On a result of 4 or higher, the target’s Evasion is halved against this attack.

features.name: Mind Dance
features.description: Mark a Stress to create a magically dazzling display that grapples the minds of nearby foes. All targets within Close range must make an Instinct Reaction Roll. For each target who failed, you gain a Fear and the Flickerfly learns one of the target’s fears.

features.name: Hallucinatory Breath
features.description: Countdown (Loop 1d6). When the Flickerfly takes damage for the first time, activate the countdown. When it triggers, the Flickerfly breathes hallucinatory gas on all targets in front of them up to Far range. Targets must succeed on an Instinct Reaction Roll or be tormented by fearful hallucinations. Targets whose fears are known to the Flickerfly have disadvantage on this roll. Targets who fail must mark a Stress and lose a Hope.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 2 > Knight Of The Realm
entries.name: Knight Of The Realm

fields.description: A decorated soldier with heavy armor and a powerful steed.
fields.motives: Run down, seek glory, show dominance

features.name: Chevalier
features.description: While the Knight is on a mount, they gain a +2 bonus to their Difficulty. When they take Severe damage, they’re knocked from their mount and lose this benefit until they’re next spotlighted.

features.name: Heavily Armored
features.description: When the Knight takes physical damage, reduce it by 3.

features.name: Cavalry Charge
features.description: If the Knight is mounted, move up to Far range and make a standard attack against a target. On a success, deal 2d8+4 physical damage and the target must mark a Stress.

features.name: For the Realm!
features.description: Mark a Stress to spotlight 1d4+1 allies. Attacks they make while spotlighted in this way deal half damage.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Adversaries > Tier 2 > Masked Thief
entries.name: Masked Thief

fields.description: A cunning thief with acrobatic skill and a flair for the dramatic.

features.name: Quick Hands
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Melee range. On a success, deal 1d8+2 physical damage and the Thief steals one item or consumable from the target’s inventory.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Environments > Tier 1 > Abandoned Grove
entries.name: Abandoned Grove

fields.description: A former druidic grove lying fallow and fully reclaimed by nature.
fields.impulses: Draw in the curious, echo the past

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Environments > Tier 1 > Ambushed
entries.name: Ambushed

fields.description: An ambush is set to catch an unsuspecting party off -guard.

features.name: Relative Strength
features.description: The Difficulty of this environment equals that of the adversary with the highest Difficulty. Who cues the ambush? What makes it clear they’re in charge?

features.name: Surprise!
features.description: The ambushers reveal themselves to the party, you gain 2 Fear, and the spotlight immediately shifts to one of the ambushing adversaries. What do the ambushers want from the party? How do their tactics in the ambush reflect that?

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Environments > Tier 1 > Ambushers
entries.name: Ambushers

fields.description: An ambush is set by the PCs to catch unsuspecting adversaries off -guard.
fields.impulses: Escape, group up, protect the most vulnerable

features.name: Relative Strength
features.description: The Difficulty of this environment equals that of the adversary with the highest Difficulty. Which adversary is the least prepared? Which one is the most?

features.name: Where Did They Come From?
features.description: When a PC starts the ambush on unsuspecting adversaries, you lose 2 Fear and the first attack roll a PC makes has advantage. What are the adversaries in the middle of doing when the ambush starts? How does this impact their approach to the fi ght?

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Environments > Tier 1 > Bustling Marketplace
entries.name: Bustling Marketplace

fields.description: The economic heart of the settlement, with local artisans, traveling merchants, and patrons across social classes.
fields.impulses: Buy low, and sell high, tempt and tantalize with wares from near and far
fields.potentialAdversaries: Guards (Bladed Guard, Head Guard), Masked Thief, Merchant

features.name: Tip the Scales
features.description: PCs can gain advantage on a Presence Roll by off ering a handful of gold as part of the interaction. Will any coin be accepted, or only local currency? How overt are the PCs in off ering this bribe?

features.name: Unexpected Find
features.description: Reveal to the PCs that one of the merchants has something they want or need, such as food from their home, a rare book, magical components, a dubious treasure map, or a magical key. What cost beyond gold will the merchant ask for in exchange for this rarity?

features.name: Sticky Fingers
features.description: A thief tries to steal something from a PC. The PC must succeed on an Instinct Roll to notice the thief or lose an item of the GM’s choice as the thief escapes to a Close distance. To retrieve the stolen item, the PCs must complete a Progress Countdown (6) to chase down the thief before the thief completes a Consequence Countdown (4) and escapes to their hideout. What drove this person to pickpocketing? Where is the thief’s hideout and how has it avoided notice?

features.name: Crowd Closes In
features.description: When one of the PCs splits from the group, the crowds shift and cut them off from the party. Where does the crowd’s movement carry them? How do they feel about being alone but surrounded?

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Environments > Tier 1 > Cliffside Ascent
entries.name: Cliffside Ascent

fields.description: A steep, rocky cliff side tall enough to make traversal dangerous.
fields.impulses: Cast the unready down to a rocky doom, draw people in with promise of what lies at the top
fields.potentialAdversaries: Construct, Deeproot Defender, Giant Scorpion, Glass Snake

features.name: The Climb
features.description: Climbing up the cliff side uses a Progress Countdown (12). It ticks down according to the following criteria when the PCs make an action roll to climb: • Critical Success: Tick down 3 • Success with Hope: Tick down 2 • Success with Fear: Tick down 1 • Failure with Hope: No advancement • Failure with Fear: Tick up 1 When the countdown triggers, the party has made it to the top of the cliff . What strange formations are the stones arranged in? What ominous warnings did previous adventurers leave?

features.name: Pitons Left Behind
features.description: Previous climbers left behind large metal rods that climbers can use to aid their ascent. If a PC using the pitons fails an action roll to climb, they can mark a Stress instead of ticking the countdown up. What do the shape and material of these pitons tell you about the previous climbers? How far apart are they from one another?

features.name: Fall
features.description: Spend a Fear to have a PC’s handhold fail, plummeting them toward the ground. If they aren’t saved on the next action, they hit the ground and tick up the countdown by 2. The PC takes 1d12 physical damage if the countdown is between 8 and 12, 2d12 between 4 and 7, and 3d12 at 3 or lower. How can you tell many others have fallen here before? What lives in these walls that might try to scare adventurers into falling for an easy meal?

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Environments > Tier 1 > Local Tavern
entries.name: Local Tavern

fields.description: A lively tavern that serves as the social hub for its town.
fields.impulses: Provide opportunities for adventurers, nurture community
fields.potentialAdversaries: Guards (Bladed Guard, Head Guard), Mercenaries (Harrier, Sellsword, Spellblade, Weaponmaster), Merchant

features.name: What’s the Talk of the Town?
features.description: A PC can ask the bartender, staff , or patrons about local events, rumors, and potential work with a Presence Roll. On a success, they can pick two of the below details to learn—or three if they critically succeed. On a failure, they can pick one and mark a Stress as the local carries on about something irrelevant. • A fascinating rumor with a connection to a PC’s background • A promising job for the party involving a nearby threat or situation • Local folklore that relates to something they’ve seen • Town gossip that hints at a community problem Who has what kind of information? What gossip do the locals start spreading about the PCs?

features.name: Sing For Your Supper
features.description: A PC can perform one time for the guests by making a Presence Roll. On a success, they earn 1d4 handfuls of gold (2d4 if they critically succeed). On a failure, they mark a Stress. What piece do you perform? What does that piece mean to you? When’s the last time you performed it for a crowd?

features.name: Mysterious Stranger
features.description: Reveal a stranger concealing their identity, lurking in a shaded booth. What do they want? What’s their impression of the PCs? What mannerisms or accessories do they have?

features.name: Someone Comes to Town
features.description: Introduce a significant NPC who wants to hire the party for something or who relates to a PC’s background. Did they know the PCs were here? What do they want in this town?

features.name: Bar Fight!
features.description: Spend a Fear to have a bar fight erupt in the tavern. When a PC tries to move through the tavern while the fight persists, they must succeed on an Agility or Presence Roll or take 1d6+2 physical damage from a wild swing or thrown object. A PC can try to activate this feature by succeeding on an action roll that would provoke tavern patrons. Who started the fi ght? What will it take to stop it?

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Environments > Tier 1 > Outpost Town
entries.name: Outpost Town

fields.description: A small town on the outskirts of a nation or region, close to a dungeon, tombs, or other adventuring destinations.
fields.impulses: Drive the desperate to certain doom, profit off of ragged hope
fields.potentialAdversaries: Jagged Knife Bandits (Hexer, Kneebreaker, Lackey, Lieutenant, Shadow, Sniper), Masked Thief, Merchant

features.name: Rumors Abound
features.description: Gossip is the fastest-traveling currency in the realm. A PC can inquire about major events by making a Presence Roll. What they learn depends on the outcome of their roll, based on the following criteria: • Critical Success: Learn about two major events. The PC can ask one follow-up question about one of the rumors and get a truthful (if not always complete) answer.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Environments > Tier 1 > Raging River
entries.name: Raging River

fields.description: A swift-moving river without a bridge crossing, deep enough to sweep away most people.
fields.impulses: Bar crossing, carry away the unready, divide the land

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Environments > Tier 2 > Cult Ritual
entries.name: Cult Ritual

fields.description: A Fallen cult assembles around a sigil of the defeated gods and a bonfire that burns a sickly shade of green.
fields.impulses: Profane the land, unite the Mortal Realm with the Circles Below

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Environments > Tier 2 > Hallowed Temple
entries.name: Hallowed Temple

fields.description: A bustling but well-kept temple that provides healing and hosts regular services, overseen by a priest or seraph.
fields.impulses: Connect the Mortal Realm with the Hallows Above, display the power of the divine, provide aid and succor to the faithful

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Environments > Tier 2 > Haunted City
entries.name: Haunted City

fields.description: An abandoned city populated by the restless spirits of eras past. Impulses: Misdirect and disorient, replay apocalypses both public and personal

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Environments > Tier 2 > Mountain Pass
entries.name: Mountain Pass

fields.description: Stony peaks that pierce the clouds, with a twisting path winding its way up and over through many switchbacks.
fields.impulses: Exact a chilling toll in supplies and stamina, reveal magical tampering, slow down travel
fields.potentialAdversaries: Beasts (Bear, Giant Eagle, Glass Snake), Chaos Skull, Minotaur Wrecker, Mortal Hunter

features.name: Engraved Sigils
features.description: Large markings and engravings have been made in the mountainside. A PC with a relevant background or Experience identifies them as weather magic increasing the power of the icy winds. A PC who succeeds on a Knowledge Roll can recall information about the sigils, potential information about their creators, and the knowledge of how to dispel them. If a PC critically succeeds, they recognize that the sigils are of a style created by ridgeborne enchanters and they gain advantage on a roll to dispel the sigils. Who laid this enchantment? Are they nearby? Why did they want the weather to be more daunting?

features.name: Avalanche
features.description: Spend a Fear to carve the mountain with an icy torrent, causing an avalanche. All PCs in its path must succeed on an Agility or Strength Reaction Roll or be bowled over and carried down the mountain. A PC using rope, pitons, or other climbing gear gains advantage on this roll. Targets who fail are knocked down the mountain to Far range, take 2d20 physical damage, and must mark a Stress. Targets who succeed must mark a Stress. How do the PCs try to weather the avalanche? What approach do the characters take to find one another when their companions go hurtling down the mountainside?

features.name: Raptor Nest
features.description: When the PCs enter the raptors’ hunting grounds, two Giant Eagles appear at Very Far range of a chosen PC, identifying the PCs as likely prey. How long has it been since the eagles last found prey? Do they have eggs in their nest, or unfledged young?

features.name: Icy Winds
features.description: Countdown (Loop 4). When the PCs enter the mountain pass, activate the countdown. When it triggers, all characters traveling through the pass must succeed on a Strength Reaction Roll or mark a Stress. A PC wearing clothes appropriate for extreme cold gains advantage on these rolls. What parts of the PC’s bodies go numb first? How do they try to keep warm as they press forward?

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Environments > Tier 3 > Burning Heart Of The Woods
entries.name: Burning Heart Of The Woods

fields.description: Thick indigo ash fi lls the air around a towering moss-covered tree that burns eternally with flames a sickly shade of blue.
fields.impulses: Beat out an uncanny rhythm for all to follow, corrupt the woods
fields.potentialAdversaries: Beasts (Bear, Glass Snake), Elementals (Elemental Spark), Verdant Defenders (Dryad, Oak Treant, Stag Knight)

features.name: Chaos Magic Locus
features.description: When a PC makes a Spellcast Roll, they must roll two Fear Dice and take the higher result. What does it feel like to work magic in this chaos-touched place? What do you fear will happen if you lose control of the spell?

features.name: The Indigo Flame
features.description: PCs who approach the central tree can make a Knowledge Roll to try to identify the magic that consumed this environment. • On a success: They learn three of the below details. On a success with Fear, they learn two. • On a failure: They can mark a Stress to learn one and gain advantage on the next action roll to investigate this environment. • Details: This is a result of Fallen magic. The corruption is spread through the ashen moss. It can be cleansed only by a ritual of nature magic with a Progress Countdown (8). What Fallen cult corrupted these woods? What have they already done with the cursed wood and sap from this tree?

features.name: Grasping Vines
features.description: Animate vines bristling with thorns whip out from the underbrush to ensnare the PCs. A target must succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll or become Restrained and Vulnerable until they break free, clearing both conditions, with a successful Finesse or Strength Roll or by dealing 10 damage to the vines. When the target makes a roll to escape, they take 1d8+4 physical damage and lose a Hope. What painful memories do the vines bring to the surface as they pierce flesh?

features.name: Charcoal Constructs
features.description: Warped animals wreathed in indigo flame trample through a point of your choice. All targets within Close range of that point must make an Agility Reaction Roll. Targets who fail take 3d12+3 physical damage. Targets who succeed take half damage instead. Are these real animals consumed by the flame or merely constructs of the corrupting magic?

features.name: Choking Ash
features.description: Countdown (Loop ). When the PCs enter the Burning Heart of the Woods, activate the countdown. When it triggers, all characters must make a Strength or Instinct Reaction Roll. Targets who fail take 4d6+5 direct physical damage. Targets who succeed take half damage. Protective masks or clothes give advantage on the reaction roll. What hallucinations does the ash induce? What incongruous taste does it possess?

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Environments > Tier 3 > Castle Siege
entries.name: Castle Siege

fields.description: An active siege with an attacking force fighting to gain entry to a fortified castle.
fields.impulses: Bleed out the will to fi ght, breach the walls, build tension
fields.potentialAdversaries: Mercenaries (Harrier, Sellsword, Spellblade, Weaponmaster), Noble Forces (Archer Squadron, Conscript, Elite Soldier, Knight of the Realm)

features.name: Secret Entrance
features.description: A PC can find or recall a secret way into the castle with a successful Instinct or Knowledge Roll. How do they get in without revealing the pathway to the attackers? Are any of the defenders monitoring this path?

features.name: Siege Weapons (Environment Change)
features.description: Consequence Countdown (6). The attacking force deploys siege weapons to try to raze the defenders’ fortifications. Activate the countdown when the siege begins (for a protracted siege, make this a long-term countdown instead). When it triggers, the defenders’ fortifications have been breached and the attackers flood inside. You gain 2 Fear, then shift to the Pitched Battle environment and spotlight it. What siege weapons are being deployed? Are they magical, mundane, or a mixture of both? What defenses must the characters overcome to storm the castle?

features.name: Reinforcements!
features.description: Summon a Knight of the Realm, a number of Tier 3 Minions equal to the number of PCs, and two adversaries of your choice within Far range of a chosen PC as reinforcements. The Knight of the Realm immediately takes the spotlight.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Environments > Tier 3 > Pitched Battle
entries.name: Pitched Battle

fields.description: A massive combat between two large groups of armed combatants.
fields.impulses: Seize people, land, and wealth, spill blood for greed and glory
fields.potentialAdversaries: Mercenaries (Sellsword, Harrier, Spellblade, Weaponmaster), Noble Forces (Archer Squadron, Conscript, Elite Soldier, Knight of the Realm)

features.name: Adrift on a Sea of Steel
features.description: Traversing a battlefield during an active combat is extremely dangerous. A PC must succeed on an Agility Roll to move at all, and can only go up to Close range on a success. If an adversary is within Melee range of them, they must mark a Stress to make an Agility Roll to move. Do the combatants mistake you for the enemy or consider you interlopers? Can you tell the diff erence between friend and foe in the fray?

features.name: Raze and Pillage
features.description: The attacking force raises the stakes by lighting a fire, stealing a valuable asset, kidnapping an important person, or killing the populace. What is valuable here? Who is most vulnerable?

features.name: War Magic
features.description: Spend a Fear as a mage from one side uses large-scale destructive magic. Pick a point on the battlefield within Very Far range of the mage. All targets within Close range of that point must make an Agility Reaction Roll. Targets who fail take 3d12+8 magic damage and must mark a Stress. What form does the attack take—fireball, raining acid, a storm of blades? What tactical objective is this attack meant to accomplish, and what comes next?

features.name: Reinforcements!
features.description: Summon a Knight of the Realm, a number of Tier 3 Minions equal to the number of PCs, and two adversaries of your choice within Far range of a chosen PC as reinforcements. The Knight of the Realm immediately takes the spotlight. Who are they targeting first? What formation do they take?

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Environments > Tier 4 > Chaos Realm
entries.name: Chaos Realm

fields.description: An otherworldly space where the laws of reality are unstable and dangerous.
fields.impulses: Annihilate certainty, consume power, defy logic

features.name: Impossible Architecture
features.description: Up is down, down is right, right is starward. Gravity and directionality themselves are in flux, and any attempt to move through this realm is an odyssey unto itself, requiring a Progress Countdown (8). On a failure, a PC must mark a Stress in addition to the roll’s other consequences. What does it feel like to move in a space so alien to the Mortal Realm? What landmark or point do you fi xate on to maintain your balance? What bizarre landmarks do you traverse on your journey?

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Environments > Tier 4 > Divine Usurpation
entries.name: Divine Usurpation

fields.description: A massive ritual designed to breach the gates of the Hallows Above and unseat the New Gods themselves.

features.name: Final Preparations
features.description: When the environment first takes the spotlight, designate one adversary as the Usurper seeking to overthrow the gods. Activate a Long-Term Countdown (8) as the Usurper assembles what they need to conduct the ritual. When it triggers, spotlight this environment to use the “Beginning of the End” feature. While this environment remains in play, you can hold up to 15 Fear. What does the Usurper still require: The heart of a High Seraph? The lodestone of an ancient waygate? The loyalty of two archenemies? The heartbroken tears of a pure soul?

features.name: Divine Blessing
features.description: When a PC critically succeeds, they can spend 2 Hope to refresh an ability normally limited by uses (such as once per rest, once per session). What god favors you as you fi ght against this usurpation? How does your renewed power reflect their influence?

features.name: Defilers Abound
features.description: Spend 2 Fear to summon 1d4+2 Fallen Shock Troops that appear within Close range of the Usurper to assist their divine siege. Immediately spotlight the Shock Troop to use a “Group Attack” action. Which High Fallen do these troops serve? Which god’s flesh do they wish to feast upon?

features.name: Godslayer
features.description: If the Divine Siege Countdown (see “Beginning of the End”) has triggered, you can spend 3 Fear to describe the Usurper slaying one of the gods of the Hallows Above, feasting upon their power and growing stronger. The Usurper clears 2 HP. Increase their Difficulty, damage, attack modifier, or give them a new feature from the slain god. Which god meets their end? What are their last words? How does the Usurper’s new stolen power manifest?

features.name: Beginning of the End
features.description: When the “Final Preparations” long-term countdown triggers, the Usurper begins hammering on the gates of the Hallows themselves. Activate a Divine Siege Countdown (10). Spotlight the Usurper to describe the Usurper’s assault and tick down this countdown by 1. If the Usurper takes Major or greater damage, tick up the countdown by 1. When it triggers, the Usurper shatters the barrier between the Mortal Realm and the Hallows Above to slay the gods and take their place. You gain a Fear for each unmarked HP the Usurper has. You can immediately use the “Godslayer” feature without spending Fear to make an additional GM move. How does the Mortal Realm writhe as the natural order is violated? What mortals witness this blasphemy from afar?

features.name: Ritual Nexus
features.description: On any failure with Fear against the Usurper, the PC must mark 1d4 Stress from the backlash of magical power.

entries.breadcrumb: Running an Adventure > Environments > Tier 4 > Imperial Court
entries.name: Imperial Court

fields.description: The majestic domain of a powerful empire, lavishly appointed with stolen treasures.
